* Chapter IX *
471
Countless devotees have appeared among
the BrГҘhmaЖ’as and the royal sages. It is just
to give a faint indication of their greatness
that a reference is made below to MaharВ¶i
SutвҲӮkВ¶Ж’a and the royal sage AmbarвҲӮВ¶a.
SUTвҲ«KВ®В°NA
MaharВ¶i SutвҲӮkВ¶Ж’a used to reside in the
DaЖ’Е’aka forest. He was a disciple of the
great Agastya. An ascetic of no mean order
possessing a remarkable spiritual glory, he
was a great devotee as well. He had to his
credit the deliverance of a VaiвҖ№ya, named
DuВ¶paЖ’ya, who had turned into a devil due
to his sins. (Skanda., Brahma., 22) SutвҲӮkВ¶Ж’a
was exclusively devoted to BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ
RГҘma. When he heard that accompained by
вҖәrвҲӮ SвҲӮtГҘ, who was no other than the Divine
Mother, вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma was coming to the DaЖ’Е’aka
forest, his joy knew no bounds. Indulging
in pious dreams of various kinds he went
forward to meet the Lord. In the ecstasy of
love he lost all consciousness of his external
surroundings. He forgot his own identity as
well as his destination, and did not know in
which direction he was proceeding and
whether there was any track before him or
not. Now he would turn round and then
advance in the same direction again: and
now he would begin to dance singing praises
of the Lord ! Hiding Himself behind a tree
the Lord of Raghus looked on the ecstatic
mood of His devotee. Observing the intensity
of the sageГӯs love the Lord, who dispels the
fear of transmigration, revealed Himself in
SutвҲӮkВ¶Ж’aГӯs heart. Obtaining a vision of the
Lord within his own heart, SutвҲӮkВ¶Ж’a now  sat
motionless in the middle of the track. A thrill
of joy ran through his whole body. The Lord
of Raghus then approached him. вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma
was extremely delighted to see him in that
rapturous mood.
The Lord of Raghus tried in many ways
to awaken the sage from his trance; but the
latter, who was enjoying the bliss of absorption
in the Lord, would not wake up. When,
however, the Lord withdrew His divine form
from the sageГӯs heart, he felt uneasy and got
up. But as soon as he opened his eyes, he
saw before him the trio of вҖәrвҲӮ SвҲӮtГҘ, LakВ¶amaЖ’a
and вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma, the abode of bliss, who is
easily distinguishable by His swarthy and
beauteous form. The reward of his austerities
stood revealed before him. His life became
supremely blessed ! (вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘmacaritamГҘnasa,
AraЖ’yakГҘЖ’Е’a).
AMBARвҲ«В®A
RГҘjarВ¶i AmbarвҲӮВ¶a was the illustrious son
of King NГҘbhГҘga, a grandson of Vaivasvata
Manu (the progenitor of the present race of
living beings). He was an emperor who ruled
over the entire terrestrial region. But knowing
as he did that all earthly glory is as unreal
as the world seen in a dream, he had
dedicated his whole life to God. His mind,
together with all his senses, remained ever
engaged in the service of God.
On one occasion the king with his
queen took the vow of observing a fast on
every EkГҘdaвҖ№вҲӮ (eleventh lunar day of the
dark and bright fortnights) for a whole year
with a view to propitiating the Lord. On the
day following the last EkГҘdaвҖ№вҲӮ of the year
worship was offered to the Lord with due
ceremony. When the king was about to
break his fast, the sage DurvГҘsГҘ called on
him with a batch of his pupils. Receiving
the sage with all honour the king invited him
to dinner. The sage accepted the royal
invitation, and went to the bank of the
YamunГҘ to perform his daily routine of
midday worship. The twelfth lunar day was
to expire only about half an hour later. A
fast observed on an EkГҘdaвҖ№вҲӮ day gets nullified
if it is not broken in course of the twelfth.
And it would be inhospitable on his part if
he broke his fast before feeding an honoured

=== PAGE 473 ===

472
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
guest like DurvГҘsГҘ. The king, therefore,
sought the advice of learned BrГҘhmaЖ’as in
this dilemma and with their concurrence
formally broke his fast by sipping a little
water out of that in which the feet of the
LordГӯs image had been washed, and awaited
the return of the sage to entertain him. After
finishing his daily routine when sage DurvГҘsГҘ
returned to the kingГӯs palace, and came to
know by supernatural power acquired through
religious austerities that the king had broken
his fast during his absence, he lost his
temper, and with an angry look spoke as
follows to the pious king, who stood before
him with joined palms as a culprit: Г¬Look
at the impertinent and sacrilegious behaviour
of this vile king, who is intoxicated with the
pride of wealth ! He is no longer a devotee
of ViВ¶Ж’u. He has begun to look upon himself
as the Deity. Having invited me as a guest,
he has broken his fast before feeding me.
Let him taste the fruit of this impertinence!Г®
With these words the irascible sage plucked
a lock of matted hair from his head and
threw it on the ground with great violence,
when immediately, like a deadly fire, a
terrible demoness of the class known as
Г«KГҰtyГҘГӯ manifested herself, and rushed at the
king, sword in hand, shaking the earth by
her heavy treads. AmbarвҲӮВ¶a, however, who
had unwavering faith in God, stood undaunted
where he was, without receding a step. He
who sees God everywhere can have no
cause for fear; why should he be afraid of
anyone?
The demoness had hardly reached
AmbarвҲӮВ¶a when the LordГӯs discus known as
SudarвҖ№ana instantaneously reduced her to
ashes, even as terrible forest fire burns down
a hissing serpent. The discus now pursued
the sage in order to wreak vengeance on
him. Unnerved by this unforeseen turn of
events the sage ran for life; but the discus
still doggedly pursued him. DurvГҘsГҘ wandered
in all directions and traversed all the fourteen
regions of the universe, but he found shelter
nowhere. No one afforded him or even
promised him help. As a last resort he went
to VaikuЖ’Вўha (the eternal abode of BhagavГҘn
ViВ¶Ж’u), and falling at the feet of the Deity
he cried out in distress, Г¬Lord ! Ignorant of
Your glory I have insulted Your devotee.
Kindly exonerate me from this fault. By
merely chanting Your name even the denizens
of hell are rid of the tortures. Therefore, pray,
forgive this fault of mine.Г®
The Lord replied as follows:Гі
Г¬O BrГҘhmaЖ’a ! I am completely under
the domination of My devotee. I am not free.
The devotee is extremely dear to Me and
rules over My heart. I hold My own Self,
and even My own Consort, Goddess LakВ¶mвҲӮ,
as of no account when compared to the
virtuous soul who is extremely devoted to
Me, and who looks upon Me as his supreme
goal. How can I forsake the devotee who
having renounced his wife, progeny, home,
family, wealth, life, and the entire prospects
of this world and the next for My sake solely
depends on Me ? Just as a devoted and
faithful wife wins over her virtuous Lord by
dint of her unadulterated love, even so the
devotee who has his mind fixed on Me and
looks upon all with the same eye wins Me
over by his unalloyed devotion. To say
nothing of perishable worlds like heaven
etc., he would not accept even the four types
of final beatitude (residence in the abode of
God, close proximity to the Deity, acquiring
a form similar to the form of the Deity and
fusion with the Deity) to which he gets
entitled by virtue of his devotion. He regards
everything as valueless when compared to
Divine Love.Г®
Finally the Lord said, Г¬BrГҘhmaЖ’a, if
you seek deliverance from this calamity, you

=== PAGE 474 ===

* Chapter IX *
473
should approach the blessed King AmbarвҲӮВ¶a
himself, and crave his pardon. Then alone
you will attain peace and happiness.Г® Thus
commanded by the Lord, DurvГҘsГҘ turned
back towards the terrestrial region.
The great devotee AmbarвҲӮВ¶a, on this
side, was passing through a peculiar state of
mind. Ever since the Discus started in pursuit
of DurvГҘsГҘ, the royal sage had been pining
out of sympathy for the latter. The BrГҘhmaЖ’a
had gone away without taking food, and the
thought that it was due to him that the
BrГҘhmaЖ’a had to run so hard for fear of
death pricked his conscience all the time.
Under such circumstances, the king felt he
had no right to take food himself. Reflecting
thus AmbarвҲӮВ¶a gave up food from that very
moment and lived on water alone. DurvГҘsГҘ
returned after a full year, but AmbarвҲӮВ¶a
budged not an inch from his vow.
DurvГҘsГҘ clutched the feet of the royal
sage as soon as he returned to his palace.
AmbarвҲӮВ¶a felt much abashed at this and humbly
prayed to SudarвҖ№ana (the discus of Lord
ViВ¶Ж’u) in the following words: Г¬If I do not
entertain in my heart the least amount of
revulsion for the sage, and if God, who is the
Self of all living beings, is pleased with me,
let yourself be appeased and deliver the sage
from this calamity.Г® The prayer appeased
SudarвҖ№ana. DurvГҘsГҘ, who was tormented with
fear, was himself again and marks of joy and
gratitude were clearly visible on his face.
(вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata IX.4-5)
*
*
*
Birth as a human being is exceptionally
rare. It is obtained as result of great merit, and
through special grace of God. It is vouchsafed
only for God-realization. The life of that being
alone is fruitful, who having obtained the
human body strives for God-realization. On
the other hand, he who seeks gratification of
self through this remains deprived of the true
blessings of human birth. For human life is
wholly joyless; there is not the least trace of
happiness anywhere in it. Contact with sense-
enjoyments, which is regarded by man as a
form of delight, is really painful inasmuch as
it throws him, again and again, into the
whirlpool of births and deaths. Therefore,
instead of regarding human life as delightful,
man should realize as speedily as possible the
object for attaining which this human existence
has been vouchsafed to him. For frail as this
human body is, no one knows which moment
it may cease to function. Hence one should
wake up to the gravity of the situation. Neither
should a man fall into the snare of enjoyments,
taking human existence to be a source of
delight, nor should he put off the practice of
adoration, considering the human body as
eternal. If this life gets frittered away due to
his own negligence, nothing but fruitless
repentance will be left open to him. The вҖәrutвҲӮ
says:Гі
Г¬If God is realized in this very life, well
and good. If, however, He is not realized
in the course of this life, one will be a great
loser.Г®* (Kena, Up. II.5).
Therefore the Lord says that having
obtained the human body one should constantly
and exclusively devote oneself to the practice
of adoration and should not forget Him even
for a moment.
The word Г«MГҘmГӯ in this verse stands
for the qualified aspect of God, and adoration
consists in devoting oneself to God in the
way indicated in the following verse, viz.,
dedicating oneГӯs mind, intellect, senses and
body etc., to God and God alone. Speedy
attainment of God is possible through the
practice of Bhajana alone, and in God-
realization alone lies the fulfilment of the
object of human existence. It is therefore that
the Lord exhorts Arjuna in this verse to
practise Bhajana or adoration of God.
*
ГҹвҖһU ГёпЈҝЕ’п¬ӮпЈҝЕ’ГӢЕ’Г• вҖҡГ Гҝв—ҠГҒSГғ Её ГёпЈҝГҒЕ’вҖһUГҠп¬ӮпЈҝЕ’ГӢГЈв—ҠвҖһUГғГӢ ГҒп¬ӮЕёГҒCГ”UвҖ”вҖ“

=== PAGE 475 ===

474
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
Knowing 
God 
as 
almighty 
and
omniscient, the supreme Ruler of all the
worlds, beyond all as well as all-formed,
absolute as well as relative, formless as well
as with form, the ocean of beauty, sweetness
and power and the very embodiment of
supreme Love, when the SГҘdhaka gets truly
acquainted with the virtues, glory, truth and
secret of God, and comes to the definite
conclusion that God alone is the supreme
object of his love, nothing in this world
retains the least charm for him. In that state
he entertains no attraction even for the
rarest enjoyment of this world. When the
SГҘdhaka reaches this stage, his mind naturally
recoils from all objects of this world and the
next, and he devotes himself with exclusive
love and supreme reverence to constant
remembrance of God and God alone. Such
loving remembrance of God becomes the
very prop of his life, and he cannot afford
to forget Him even for a moment. The
devotee who has attained this state is referred
to here as Г«ManmanГҘВЁГӯ (he whose mind is
absorbed in God).
A devotee of God is he who resigns
Himself to the will of God, taking Him to
be the supreme goal, the sole supporter and
lord, nay, the supreme refuge and the best
friend and protector; who is ever contented
with every dispensation of God; who carries
out his commands; who keeps his mind,
intellect and senses absorbed in hearing,
chanting and remembering His names, virtues,
glory and stories etc., and does everything
for the sake of His pleasure.
Visiting temples and offering proper
worship to the sacred image of God, installing
an image of oneГӯs beloved Deity in oneГӯs
own home, if convenient, and offering  worship
to the same with due ceremony, faith and
love; installing a mental image of the Deity
in the cavity of oneГӯs own heart or in the
open space just in front of the SГҘdhaka, and
offering mental worship to the same, showing
due reverence to His commandments, to the
scenes of His sacred sports, and to His portraits
etc., keeping oneself engaged in activities
connected with His service and worshipping
Him through disinterested performance of
sacrifices; looking upon oneГӯs parents and
other elders and BrГҘhmaЖ’as in particular, and
all living beings in general, as so many
manifestations of God, or knowing God to
be present in all as the inner witness, and
offering them due worship and honour, and
sincerely trying to gratify and render good
offices to them with mind, body and material
resources: all these acts are included in what
is known as the worship of God.
Revealing the glory of Bhajana in the foregoing verses, Arjuna was eventually exhorted
to practise the same. The Lord, therefore, now delineates the process of Bhajana, or self-
surrender to God, and concludes the Chapter.
в—ҠГЈв—ҠЕёГҠ Г·п¬Ӯ в—ҠjQВ§ГҠпЈҝ в—ҠlГҠВЎГӢ в—ҠГҠВў Еёв—ҠSвҲ‘ГҲВ§LВ§вҖ“
в—ҠГҠГ”в—ҠпЈҝп¬ӮГ’cГҝГҒвҖҡ ГҝГҲГ„Г п¬ӮГ’п¬Ӯв—ҠГҠГ в—ҠГҠЕёВў в—ҠГ В¬вҒ„UГҠГҝГЎГҠвҖ”H 34H
в—ҠГЈв—ҠЕёГҠвҖ” Г·п¬Ӯ fix your mind on Me; в—ҠjQВ§вҖ”Г” be devoted to Me; в—ҠlГҠВЎГӢ (Г·п¬Ӯ) worship Me;
в—ҠГҠв—ҠЛңГ” Еёв—ҠSвҲ‘ГҲВ§LВ§Г” (and) make obeisance to Me; в—ҠГҠв—ҠЛңГ” to Me; вҲһп¬Ӯ alone; вҲһcГҝГҒвҖҡГ” you shall come;
ГҝГҲГ„Г п¬ӮГҠ linking (with Me); вҲһп¬Ӯв—ҠЛңГ” thus; вҖўГҠГ в—ҠГҠЕёв—ҠЛңГ” your self; в—ҠГ В¬вҒ„UГҠГҝГЎГҠвҖ”Г” solely depending on Me.
Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me and make obeisance to
Me; thus uniting yourself to Me and entirely depending on Me, you shall come to
Me.                                                                 (34)

=== PAGE 476 ===

* Chapter IX *
475
The word Г«MГҘmГӯ in this verse refers to
that integral Divinity who is the creator,
destroyer and controller of the whole universe,
all-sustaining, all-powerful, all-pervading, all-
knowing, the universal friend and well-wisher,
the abode of all virtues, the supreme and best
Person; who is both absolute and Г«SaguЖ’aГӯ
as well as with form and without form; who
sustains all as ViВ¶Ж’u,  creates all as BrahmГҘ,
and destroys all as Rudra; who appears in
our midst from age to age in various divine
forms such as the Fish, Tortoise, Boar, Man-
Lion, вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma, вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and so on, and
enacts wonderful sports in this world; and
who manifests Himself in various forms as
desired by His devotees, and takes them
under His protection. And paying due homage
in a devout and reverent spirit with oneГӯs
mind, speech and body to the God with or
without form, to an image or portrait of the
Deity, to His blessed feet or marks thereof, to
the scriptures expounding the truth, secret,
love, glory and enchanting sports of the Lord,
to saints and holy men who are the living
images of God, nay, to all living beings of
the universe, taking them as so many
manifestations of God or considering God
to be present in them as the inner witnessГі
all this is included in offering obeisance to
God.
The word Г«Г…tmГҘnamГӯ (self) in this verse
stands for the body, including the mind,
intellect and senses. And applying them all to
God in the way indicated above is what is
meant by linking the self with Him.
Complete dependence on  God consists
in thus offering everything to Him in the
manner suggested above, and looking upon
Him as the supreme object worth attaining,
supreme goal, supreme refuge and oneГӯs all-
in-all.
The word  Г«EvaГӯ has been used for the
sake of emphasis. The Lord thereby seeks
to emphasize the fact that by striving on
the lines suggested above Arjuna would
undoubtedly attain Him. And obtaining a
direct vision of God during oneГӯs very life-
time, knowing Him in reality and entering
His very being, or ascending to His divine
abode, living in close proximity with Him, or
obtaining a form similar to the Divine Form:
all these are included in God-realization.

Thus, in the UpaniВ¶ad sung by the Lord, the science of Brahma,
the scripture of Yoga, the Dialogue between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a
and Arjuna, ends the ninth Chapter entitled
Г¬The Yoga of the Sovereign Science
and the Sovereign Secret.Г®

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
Title of the
Chapter
Summary of
the Chapter
Лҷ
вҖәrвҲӮmadbhagavad-GвҲӮtГҘ
Chapter X
The chapter mainly deals with the special manifestations of God; hence it
has been given the title of VibhВөutiyoga, or the Yoga of Divine
manifestations.
In the opening verse of this chapter the Lord undertakes to confide once more the
supreme gospel and invites Arjuna to hear the same. In the second and third He describes
His own glory under the name of Г«YogaГӯ and indicates the fruit of its
knowledge. Briefly enumerating His special manifestations in verses 4 to 6,
He gives out the fruit of knowing them as well as His Yoga in the seventh.
Detailing in the eighth and ninth the mode of worship practised by His wise devotees
possessing exclusive love for Him, He describes the fruit of such worship in the tenth and
eleventh. Thereafter, extolling the Lord in verses 12 to 15 Arjuna requests Him in verses 16
to 18 to describe in detail His special manifestations and power of Yoga once more. Pointing
out the range of His divine manifestations as infinite and undertaking to name only the chief
among them in the nineteenth, the Lord recounts them in verses 20 to 39. Reiterating the fact
that the range of His divine manifestations is infinite, the Lord winds up this topic in the
fortieth. And the chapter is concluded with a description of His Divine glory under the name
of Г«YogaГӯ in the forty-first and forty-second verses.
The exposition of JВӨГҘna and VijВӨГҘna (the Knowledge of NirguЖ’a and SaguЖ’a Brahma)
contained in chapters VII to IX having become too abstruse, the Lord
introduces this chapter in order to expound the truth clearly once more in a
different way. In the very opening verse of this chapter the Lord undertakes
to discuss the same subject.

* Chapter X *

The supreme word (Paramam VacaВЁ)
is that which unravels the mystery of the

the truth relating to Bhakti or devotion to
Him is exceptionally difficult to understand;
hence he should hear it with great attention,
reverence and love, considering it most essential
to hear it again and again.
By using the adjective Г«PrвҲӮyamГҘЖ’ГҘyaГӯ

the love you bear in your heart for Me is
exceedingly great, and you listen to My

relishing them as nectar. It is therefore that
without the least hesitation, and even

to you the mystery of My most secret
virtues, glory and truth. All this is due to
your love.Г® And the use of the word

and devotion had filled the LordГӯs heart with
solicitude for the latterГӯs welfare. Therefore,
whatever the Lord proposed to tell him was
naturally an expression of His solicitude for
his welfare.
Taking up the subject proposed to be discussed in this chapter, the Lord first gives in
the following five verses a short description of His Yogic power and divine glory.

Neither gods nor the great sages know the secret of My birth (i.e., My manifestation

He who knows Me in reality as birthless and without beginning, and as the
supreme Lord of the Universe, he, undeluded among men, is purged of all sins.  (3)
protector of all. And to grasp this truth

The latter half of this verse seeks to
bring out the fruit of knowing God as unborn,
without beginning and the supreme Lord of
the Universe as explained above. The intention
is to show that of all men in this world, he
alone who possesses a correct knowledge of
GodГӯs glory, as indicated above, knows God

Again, he alone who knows God in reality
is Г«undeluded among menГӯ; the rest are all
deluded. And he who has thoroughly grasped
the reality of God naturally devotes the precious
moments of his life to the constant practice of
adoration in every way (XV.19); like sensually-
minded people he does not remain engrossed
in worldly enjoyments, taking them to be a
source of delight. Hence being thoroughly
purged of all sins of his present birth and past
lives, he easily succeeds in realizing God.
Though Г«manifesting Himself in various
forms through His own YogamГҘyГҘ (divine
potency), God is really birthless (IV.6), and
is not born like other earthly beings. He
simply plays the role of being born in order
to gratify His devotees and establish
righteousness on earth. To grasp this truth
correctly with unquestioning faith and reverence
is to know God as birthless. Even so, God
is the beginning or ultimate cause of all, but
has no beginning Himself; He is eternal and
dateless, and like other things he cannot be
said to have started His career from a
particular point of time. And to apprehend
this truth properly with full faith and reverence
is to know God as without beginning. Again,
whatever regional gods there are, such as
Indra, VaruЖ’a, Yama, PrajГҘpati and others,
who are held to be divine in rank, God is
the supreme Lord of them all; He is the

creator, destroyer, supporter, nourisher and

=== PAGE 480 ===

* Chapter X *
479
The function of the mind which determines
what ought to be done and what ought not to
be done, what is worthy of acceptance and
what is worth rejecting, what is good and what
is evil, is called Г«BuddhiГӯ or reason.
The term Г«JВӨГҘnaГӯ covers every form of
knowledge, from ordinary worldly knowledge
to the knowledge of the reality of God.
Regarding worldly enjoyments, which
appear everlasting and delightful to all
sensually-minded men, as transient, momentary
and sorrowful, and refusing to be infatuated
by them is called Г«AsammohaГӯ or unclouded
understanding.
Harbouring evil intentions, doing an ill
turn, usurping oneГӯs possessions, offering insult,
causing hurt, using harsh or abusive language,
indulging in calumny or malicious gossip,
practising incendiarism, administering poison,
committing murder and causing direct or
indirect harm,Гіall these are offences which
easily provoke anger and retaliation. Total
absence of the spirit of retaliation even under
such provocation and notwithstanding the
power to retaliate, and seeking exemption for
the offender from all punishment in this as
well as in the next world is what is known as
forbearance.
Г«VeracityГӯ means representing in sweet
and loving words what has been actually
seen with the eyes, heard through the ears, or
apprehended through the mind exactly as it
has been seen, heard or apprehended.
Г«DamaГӯ consists in exercising restraint
over the senses running after sense-objects,
subjugating them and checking their
waywardness; whereas Г«вҖәamaГӯ means subduing
the mind by exercising full control over the
same.
Г«SukhaГӯ denotes joys of all kinds derived
from contact with agreeable objects and the
withdrawal of disagreeable ones. Even so
Г«sorrowГӯ covers all forms of suffering* caused
by separation from agreeable objects and
contact with disagreeable ones.
Г«BhavaГӯ means coming into existence of
the entire universe consisting of animate and
inanimate beings at the time of creation; and
its disappearance at the time of universal
destruction is indicated by the word Г«AbhГҘvaГӯ.
вҖўГҒвҖһВўUвҖҡГҠ вҖҡв—ҠГғГҠ ГғГҲГҒCГ”USГғВ¬ГҠпЈҝ Е’ГҠЕёВў ГҝвүҘГҠГҠпЈҝЛҳГҝвүҘГҠвҖ”Г”вҖ“
Г·п¬ӮГҒГЈГғ Г·ГҠп¬ӮГҠ Г·ГҚГғГҠЕёГҠВў в—ҠГ»ГҠ вҲһп¬Ӯ В¬ГҺГ•ГҒГҮп¬ӮЕ“ГҠвҖ”Г”H 5H
вҖҷГҲГҒhвҖ” reason; ГҙГҠГҠЕёв—ҠЛңГ” right knowledge; вҖўвҖҡГӘв—ҠГҠпЈҝвҖһUвҖ” clear understanding; ЛҮГҠв—ҠГҠГ” forbearance; вҖҡГ Гҝв—ҠЛңГ”
truthfulness; Е’в—ҠвҖ”Г” control of the senses; вүҘГҠв—ҠвҖ” control of the mind; вҖҡГҲПҖв—ҠЛңГ” joy; Е’ГҲвҖ”ПҖв—ҠЛңГ” sorrow; Г·п¬ӮвҖ”Г”
evolution; вҖўГ·ГҠп¬ӮвҖ”Г” dissolution; Г·Гҝв—ҠЛңГ” fear; Гё and; вҖўГ·Гҝв—ҠЛңГ” fearlessness; вҲһп¬Ӯ alone; Гё and; вҖўГҒвҖһВўUвҖҡГҠ
non-violence; вҖҡв—ҠГғГҠ equanimity; ГғГҲГҒCГ”UвҖ” contentment; ГғВ¬вҖ” austerity; Е’ГҠЕёв—ҠЛңГ” charity; ГҝвүҘГҠвҖ” fame;
вҖўГҝвүҘГҠвҖ” obloquy; Г·п¬ӮГҒГЈГғ emanate; Г·ГҠп¬ӮГҠвҖ” traits; Г·ГҚГғГҠЕёГҠв—ҠЛңГ” in creatures; в—ҠГ»ГҠвҖ” from Me; вҲһп¬Ӯ as well;
В¬ГҺГ•ГҒГҮп¬ӮЕ“ГҠвҖ” diverse.
Reason, right knowledge, clear understanding, forbearance, veracity, control over
the senses and mind, joy and sorrow, evolution and dissolution, fear and fearlessness,
non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and obloquyГіthese
diverse traits of creatures emanate from Me alone.                                (4, 5)
* Suffering has been classified in Hindu вҖәГҘstras under three heads: viz., (i) Г…dhibhautika or that
caused through the agency of men, beasts, birds, insects, moths and other creatures; (ii) Г…dhidaivika or
that resulting from natural calamities such as drought, excessive rainfall, earthquake, stroke of lightning
and famine etc., and (iii) Г…dhyГҘtmika or that caused by distemper of the body, senses or mind.

=== PAGE 481 ===

480
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
Agitation or perturbation of the mind
caused by impending harm or cause of death
is called Г«BhayaГӯ (fear); and total absence of
fear resulting from a sense of the omnipresence
of God, or from any other cause, is known as
Г«AbhayaГӯ (fearlessness).
Г«AhiвүҲsГҘГӯ or non-violence means non
infliction of suffering in any form and at any
time even in a small degree on any being
whatsoever in thought, word and deed.
Г«SamatГҘГӯ or equanimity denotes constant
equipoise of mind, free from partiality and
prejudice, in the midst of objects, actions and
incidents etc., which are likely to throw one
off oneГӯs balance such as joy and sorrow,
gain and loss, victory and defeat, praise and
blame, honour and ignominy, friend and foe,
etc.
Г«TuВ¶ВўiГӯ 
or 
contentment 
is 
that
uncomplaining attitude of mind which
reconciles itself to every lot, taking it as the
outcome of oneГӯs own good or evil destiny
or as a decree of Providence.
Г«TapasГӯ or austerity consists in enduring
hardships for the sake of oneГӯs Dharma or
duty, and Г«DГҘnaГӯ or charity consists in
surrendering oneГӯs own proprietary rights for
the benefit of others. Even so, good and bad
reputation are known as Г«YaвҖ№aГӯ and Г«AyaвҖ№aГӯ,
respectively.
All these characteristics and feelings
existing in diverse creatures and varying
according to the temperament of each owe
their very existence to His backing, power
and presence. This is what is meant by the
second half of verse 5.
It may be observed here that while in
the above two verses the antitheses of Г«SukhaГӯ,
Г«BhavaГӯ, Г«AbhayaГӯ and Г«YaвҖ№aГӯ, viz., Г«DuВЁkhaГӯ,
Г«AbhГҘvaГӯ, Г«BhayaГӯ and Г«AyaвҖ№aГӯ have been
spoken of as proceeding from God, those of
Г«KВ¶amГҘГӯ, Г«SatyaГӯ, Г«DamaГӯ and Г«AhiвүҲsГҘГӯ etc.,
have not been so mentioned. How is that to
be accounted for ? The reason is not far to
seek. It will be seen that entities like sorrow,
dissolution, fear and obloquy etc., appear in
this world just in order to enable beings to
work out their evil destiny. Their emanation
from God, who dispenses the fruit of our
actions and controls the universe, is therefore
quite in order. Vices and evil propensities
like anger, mendacity, self-indulgence and
violence etc., which are the antitheses of
forbearance, veracity, sense-control and
nonviolence etc., on the other hand, do not
proceed from God. The GвҲӮtГҘ attributes these,
elsewhere, to Г«desireГӯ, born of ignorance (vide
III.37), and exhorts us to renounce them
along with their root cause. That is why the
antitheses of virtues and noble propensities
like veracity etc., have not been mentioned in
the present verse.
в—ҠвҖһUВ·ВёГҝвҖ” вҖҡ# В¬ГҚп¬ӮпЈҝВё ГёГ п¬ӮГҠвҒ„UГҠпЈҝ в—ҠЕёп¬ӮSГғГ•ГҠГ”вҖ“
в—ҠjГҠп¬ӮГҠ в—ҠГҠЕёвҖҡГҠ ВЎГҠГғГҠ ГҝпЈҝВ·ГҠВў вҲӮUГҠпЈҝвҲ‘В§ Гҹв—ҠГҠвҖ” В¬ЛқВЎГҠвҖ”Г”H 6H
в—ҠвҖһUВ·ВёГҝвҖ” вҖҡ# the seven great seers; В¬ГҚп¬ӮпЈҝВё ГёГ п¬ӮГҠвҒ„UвҖ” their four elders (Sanaka and others); в—ҠЕёп¬ӮвҖ”
the fourteen Manus or progenitors of mankind (such as SvГҘyambhuva and his successors);
ГғГ•ГҠГ” and; в—ҠЕ’ЛңГ”Г·ГҠп¬ӮГҠвҖ” (who are all) devoted to Me; в—ҠГҠЕёвҖҡГҠвҖ” ВЎГҠГғГҠвҖ” were born of My will; ГҝпЈҝВ·ГҠв—ҠЛңГ” from
whom; вҲӮUГҠпЈҝвҲ‘пЈҝВ§ in the world; Гҹв—ҠГҠвҖ” В¬ЛқВЎГҠвҖ”Г” all these creatures (have come into being).
The seven great seers, their four elders (Sanaka and others), and the fourteen
Manus or progenitors of mankind (such as SvГҘyambhuva and his successors), who are
all devoted to Me, were born of My will; from them all these creatures in the world
have come into being.                                                                              (6)

Г¬And those who dwell on the above
sentiments of celestial seers*, are recognized
as В°RВ¶is or seers. They are seven in number
and are possessed of the following seven virtues;
hence they are called SaptarВ¶is. They are all
long-lived and composers of Vedic Mantras
or hymns, possessed of divine powers and
transcendent vision and profound in merits,
learning and age. They are credited with an
intuitive or direct perception of Dharma or

These seven seers or SaptarВ¶is, as they

These seven seers are advocates of the worldly ways of life. Their views and mode of life are

В·вүҲЛңUГ”вҲ‘В§в—ҠГҠВёГҒГ·вҒ„UГғГҠ ГҒЕёГ ГҝВў  вүҘГҠГҠГҒвҲӮUЕёГҠпЈҝ ВӘГҺвҖһUв—ҠпЈҝГҒЕ“ЕёвҖ”вҖ“ ГғГҲГҖГҝГ’вҲҡГҝВёп¬ӮвҖһUвҒ„UГҒГЈГғ Sв—Ҡ вҖўЕ’ГҺCГ”пЈҝвҖ” вҲ‘В§в—ҠВёвҖһпЈҝUГғГҲГҒГ·вҖ”H

(VГҘyu-PurГҘЖ’a 61.95-97)
Г¬These great seers are constantly engaged in the six pursuits enjoined upon a BrГҘhmaЖ’a, viz., the
study and teaching of the Vedas, performing sacrifices and officiating as priests in sacrifices performed by
other and bestowing and accepting gifts. They maintain Gurukulas or seminaries in their hermitages for
imparting instruction to BrahmacГҘrвҲӮs and marry and preserve the sacred fire only for the continuity of the
race. They have matrimonial and other relations only with those who belong to the same VarЖ’a or caste,
and enjoy irreproachable objects of their own creation. They maintain wife and children, own cattle and
other possession, and live both within and without the world of matter. At the beginning of every Yuga
it is these seers who establish, for the first time, the insitution of VarЖ’as and Г…вҖ№ramas (the division of society
into various grades and stages of life) again and again.
вҖЎ All these seven seers are PrajГҘpatis of great religious austerity, possessing uncommon spiritual glow
and wisdom. Being creators of beings, they are known as the Г«Seven BrahmГҘsГӯ (MahГҘ., вҖәГҘnti., 208-3,4,5).

482
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
The words Г«Sapta MaharВ¶ayaВЁГӯ in the
present verse cannot be interpreted to mean
the seven seers of the present Manvantara,
viz., ViвҖ№vГҘmitra, Jamadagni, BharadvГҘja,
Gautama, Atri, VasiВ¶Вўha and KaвҖ№yapa, because
besides Atri and VasiВ¶Вўha none of these was
born of the will either of God Himself or of
BrahmГҘ (the Creator). Therefore, instead of
(1) MARвҲ«CI
This seer is held to be a part manifestation of a fraction of God. He has many wives, the chief of them
being SambhВөuti, a daughter of PrajГҘpati, DakВ¶a, and DharmavratГҘ, the daughter of a BrГҘhmaЖ’a named
Dharma. He has a large stock of descendants. MaharВ¶i KaвҖ№yapa is one of his own sons. A portion of the
Padma-PurГҘЖ’a was recited to him by BrahmГҘ. Almost all the PurГҘЖ’as, the MahГҘbhГҘrata and the Vedas too
contain copious references to him. BrahmГҘ delivered the Brahma-PurГҘЖ’a first of all to this sage. He is
constantly engaged in the work of creation and maintenance of the world. Detailed accounts of this sage are
to be found in the VГҘyu, Skanda, Agni, Padma, MГҘrkaЖ’Е’eya and ViВ¶Ж’u-PurГҘЖ’as and the MahГҘbhГҘrata etc.
(2) AГғGIRГ…
He is a seer possessed of uncommon spiritual glow. He has many wives, three of whom are more
prominent. Of these SurВөupГҘ, daughter of MarвҲӮci, bore BГҰhaspati; SvarГҘt, daughter of sage Kardama, gave
birth to five sons, viz., Gautama, VГҘmadeva and others; and PathyГҘ, daughter of SvГҘyambhuva Manu,
brought forth three sons, ViВ¶Ж’u and others. (VГҘyu-PurГҘЖ’a. LXV.); whereas Г…treyвҲӮ, daughter of Agni, gave
birth of many sons collectively known as the Г…вҲҶgirasas (Brahma-PurГҘЖ’a). According to some scriptures
BГҰhaspati was born of his wife вҖәubhГҘ, (MahГҘbhГҘrata).
(3) ATRI
This seer has his abode in the south. AnasВөuyГҘ, who is famous for her feminine chastity, is his consort.
AnasВөuyГҘ is a co-uterine sister of BhagavГҘn Kapila, being born of Kardama and DevahВөuti. BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ
RГҘma accepted the hospitality of this great lady during His sojourn in the forest. Adorning the divine
Mother, вҖәrвҲӮ SвҲӮtГҘ, with ornaments and clothes of various kinds, AnasВөuyГҘ also delivered to her her historic
message on the duties of a virtuous woman.
 Commanded by BrahmГҘ to propagate the race, the great sage Atri, who is the most prominent among
all expounders of the Vedas, repaired to the В°RkВ¶a mountain with his wife, AnasВөuyГҘ, and devoted himself
to the practice of austerities. Both Atri and his wife are great devotees of God. They performed rigorous
penance and sought direct communion with God as a reward for their penance. Surrendering themselves to
God, the Lord of the entire universe, they engaged themselves in constant thought of the Lord. The crown
of their head emitted the fire of Yoga, which began to burn the three worlds. Pleased with their penance,
the holy Trinity, BrahmГҘ, ViВ¶Ж’u and вҖәaвҲҶkara, simultaneously appeared before these devotees to bless them.
Obtaining a direct vision of the three deities all at once, the seer and his wife had the object of their life
fulfilled and they began to extol the Lord in a voice choked with emotion. The Lord insisted on their asking
a boon. Having been commanded by BrahmГҘ to procreate, Atri replied, Г¬I invoked the Lord and sought His
vision for obtaining a son, and I find all the three of You before me! You are all beyond human conception.
How is it, then, that You have condescended to bless me thus? Pray, tell me.Г® Hearing these words of Atri,
the three gods smiled and said, Г¬O sage, you are of unfailing resolve. We three are the same as the object
of your contemplation, three aspects of the same truth. You will have three sons, who will be part
manifestations of Ourselves. As for yourself you have all your desires already fulfilled.Г® Having uttered these
words, the Trinity disappeared and thereafter incarnated themselves as the sons of Atri. A part of BhagavГҘn
ViВ¶Ж’u appeared as DattГҘtreya, a part of BrahmГҘ took the form of Candra (the moon-god), while a part of
вҖәiva incarnated itself as the sage DurvГҘsГҘ. Such is the glory of devotion! Those very gods who are beyond
human conception assumed the forms of human children and sported in the lap of AnasВөuyГҘ (VГҘlmвҲӮkiГӯs
RГҘmГҘyaЖ’a, VanakГҘЖ’Е’a, вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata, Bk. IV).
(4) PULASTYA
He is a great ascetic, pious and full of spiritual glow. He is an eminent Master of Yoga and a great
adept in this branch of Knowledge. Once upon a time when sage ParГҘвҖ№ara was performing a grand sacrifice

(5) PULAHA
This great seer is possessed of great prowess and wisdom. Obtaining divine knowledge from
sage Sanandana, he imparted the same to sage Gautama. Through his wives, KВ¶amГҘ, daughter of
PrajГҘpati DakВ¶a and Gati, daughter of sage Kardama, he got many children (vide KВөurma, ViВ¶Ж’u and вҖәrвҲӮmad

(6) KRATU
This great seer also possesses exceptional spiritual glow. He married KriyГҘ, daughter of sage  Kardama,
and Sannati, daughter of PrajГҘpati DakВ¶a. He begot eight thousand В°RВ¶is collectively known as the
VГҘlakhilyas, who stand facing the sun-god near His chariot and move with the latter uttering his praises.
The PurГҘЖ’as contain many references to these В°RВ¶is (vide вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata, Bk. IV; ViВ¶Ж’u-PurГҘЖ’a, Pt.I).
(7) VASIВ®В§THA
The austerity, spiritual glow, forbearance and piety of this great seer are universally known. The
PurГҘЖ’as record divergent accounts of his birth, which are all correct, relating as they do to different Kalpas
or cycles of creation. The name of his wife is ArundhatвҲӮ, who is an exceptionally virtuous woman and the
foremost of all loyal and devoted wives. VasiВ¶Вўha was the family priest of the Kings of the Solar Race. It was
the temptation of seeing with his own eyes, and living in the holy company of, BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma, the
ideal and perfect Man, that induced him to accept this office, and he constantly strove for the welfare of the
solar race. Obtaining вҖәrвҲӮ RГҘma as his own disciple, he deemed the mission of his life as having been fulfilled.
It is said that once there was a controversy between VasiВ¶Вўha and ViвҖ№vГҘmitra. The subject of discussion
was: Which is superiorГіausterity or SatsaвҲҶga (the company of holy men)? VasiВ¶Вўha maintained the superiority
of SatsaвҲҶga, while ViвҖ№vГҘmitra declared austerity as of greater value. Finally the question was referred to вҖәeВ¶a,
the serpent-god, for arbitration. Hearing the point at issue BhagavГҘn вҖәeВ¶a replied, Г¬O blessed ones, both of
you can see that the weight of the entire globe rests on my head. If either of you relieves me of this burden
for a short while, I may have breathing time to think over the question and settle your dispute.Г® ViвҖ№vГҘmitra
had great faith in his own austerities; he, therefore, sought to lift up the earth by parting with the fruit of ten
thousand years of his penance, but in vain. The earth began to quake. Thereupon VasiВ¶Вўha easily lifted the
earth by parting with the fruit of only half a second of the SatsaвҲҶga practised by him, and remained standing
with the load for a long time. ViвҖ№vГҘmitra then enquired of the arbitrator: How is it that you do not pronounce
your decision even though so much time has elapsed? Г¬O great seer !Г® вҖәeВ¶a smilingly replied, Г¬the question
stands decided by itself without my arbitration. When ten thousand yearsГӯ penance cannot equal half-a-
secondГӯs SatsaвҲҶga, you can yourself decide which is superior.Г® Knowing thus the glory of SatsaвҲҶga, the two
sages returned to their abodes, much delighted at heart.
VasiВ¶Вўha got this name because he is endowed with Vasu, or the eight siddhis (occult powers), and

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
as the four Г«SanasГӯ (embodiments of austerity),
as the result of the ceaseless penance done
by me at the beginning of creation with the
object of creating the different worlds. They
fully expounded the true nature of the soul,
which had been lost to the world during the
dissolution at the end of the preceding Kalpa,
whereby seers realized the truth in their own
heart.Г® (вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata II.vii.5)*.
Here it may be observed that Sanaka
and others had no issue at all, whereas the
present verse says that all these creatures in
the world have descended from the seers and
Manus mentioned therein. Therefore, the
construction put upon the words Г«CatvГҘraВЁ
PВөurveГӯ presents a contradiction in terms. But
this contradiction is only apparent, and not
real. For the Sanaka brothers, being the pioneers
of the path of Renunciation (NivГҰtti), are the
teachers of the whole universe. Hence all
those who follow the path shown by them
are in a way their disciples and may therefore
be taken to be their progeny.
In the course of a cosmic day as many
as fourteen Manus appear and hold office in
succession, and the tenure of office of each
Manu is called a Г«ManvantaraГӯ. A Manvantara
consists of a little more than seventy-one
Г«CaturyugвҲӮsГӯ or rounds of the four Yugas
(Satyayuga, TretГҘ, DvГҘpara and Kaliyuga).
Reckoned in human years, a Manvantara
extends to 30,67,20,000 odd years; whereas
according to the computation of the celestials,
it consists of 8,52,000 odd years (ViВ¶Ж’u-PurГҘЖ’a
1.3).вҖ“ Each Manvantara has its own SaptarВ¶is
or group of seven seers for regulating the
operation of Dharma or the Divine Law and
maintaining the universe. With the change of
a Manu at the conclusion of a Manvantara,
other functionaries of the universe, such as
the seven seers, gods, Indra, and the sons of
Manu also change. The names of the Manus
of the present Kalpa are: SvГҘyambhuva,
SvГҘrociВ¶a, Uttama, TГҘmasa, Raivata CГҘkВ¶uВ¶a,
Vaivasvata, 
SГҘvarЖ’i, 
DakВ¶asГҘvarЖ’i,
BrahmasГҘvarЖ’i, DharmasГҘvarЖ’i, RudrasГҘvarЖ’i,
is the best of all householders. The enemies of man in the shape of lust, anger, greed, infatuation etc., could
not even enter the precincts of his hermitage. Though fully able to retaliate, he felt no provocation and
did not inflict the least injury on ViвҖ№vГҘmitra, the murderer of his hundred sons. Pleased with him, Lord вҖәiva
granted to him lordship over the BrГҘhmaЖ’as. Among those who possess true knowledge of SanГҘtana Dharma,
the name of VasiВ¶Вўha deserves to be cited first of all. Detailed accounts of his life appear in the RГҘmГҘyaЖ’a,
MahГҘbhГҘrata, DevвҲӮ-BhГҘgavata, the ViВ¶Ж’u, Matsya, VГҘyu, вҖәiva and LiвҲҶga PurГҘЖ’as and other books.

вҖ“
According to SВөuryasiddhГҘnta (an old and authoritative treatise on Astronomy) the duration of
a Manvantara etc., has been computed as below:Гі
A CaturyugвҲӮ (one full round of the Satya, TretГҘ, DvГҘpara and Kali Yugas) consists of 43,20,000 human
years or 12,000 celestial years. This period is also called a Г«MahГҘyugaГӯ. Seventy-one rounds of such
MahГҘyugas go to make a Г«ManvantaraГӯ. A period equal in duration to a Satyayuga or 17,28,000 human
years intervenes the expiration of each Manvantara and the commencement of another, and is technically
known as a SandhyГҘ or twilight. During this SandhyГҘ occurring at the end of each Manvantara the earth
goes under water. In every Kalpa or Cosmic day there are fourteen Manvantaras with an equal number of
twilights. Besides these, there is one more twilight of the same duration at the beginning of each Kalpa.
In this way the fourteen Manvantaras comprised in each Kalpa consist of 71 CaturyugвҲӮs each, besides 15
twilights each of the duration of a Satyayuga. The 14 Manvantaras thus comprise in all 14x71=994
MahГҘyugas plus 15 twilights, which go to make another 6 MahГҘyugas. In this way each Kalpa comprises
994+6=1,000 MahГҘyugas. The following figures represent the duration of each of these divisions of them:Гі

* Chapter X *
485
DevasГҘvarЖ’i, and IndrasГҘvarЖ’i.* At the end
of a Kalpa consisting of fourteen Manvantaras
the entire group of Manus changes.
All these seers and Manus are full of
reverence and love for God; it is for this
reason that the qualification Г«MadbhГҘvГҘВЁГӯ
has been used with reference to them.
Although the scriptures speak of the
seven seers as well as of Sanaka, Sanandana
and others as having been born of the mind
of BrahmГҘ, the Lord refers to them here as
having been born of His own will. This
discrepancy can be explained by the fact that
it is God Himself who assumes the form of
BrahmГҘ for the creation of the universe.
Therefore, there is no contradiction when the
Lord speaks of those born of BrahmГҘГӯs mind
as having been born of His own will.

1,20,00,000
Kalpa is only another name for a cosmic day, and each cosmic day is followed by a cosmic night of the
same length. The full length of BrahmГҘГӯs life is a hundred years according to this measure. This period of time
is technically known as Г«ParaГӯ. Having completed half of his period, BrahmГҘ is now passing through the other
half. The present time represents the first day or Kalpa of his fifty-first year. Since the beginning of this Kalpa
six Manvantaras with their SandhyГҘs have already passed; whereas the number of SandhyГҘs, including the one
occurring at the beginning of the Kalpa, that have elapsed so far is seven. The present is the seventh or
Vaivasvata Manvantara, of which 27 Caturyugas have passed. We are now passing through the twilight at the
beginning of the Kaliyuga of the 28th Caturyuga. (SВөuryasiddhГҘnta, MadhyamГҘdhikГҘra, 15 to 24).
Up to the SaвүҲvat year 2074 (2016 A.D.) of the Vikrama Era, Kaliyuga has completed 5,118 years.
The SandhyГҘ occurring at the beginning of each Kaliyuga extends to 36,000 years. According to this
calculation 30,952 years of the SandhyГҘ itself remain to be completed.
* For a detailed account of these Manus see Chapters 1, 5 and 13 of Bk.VIII of вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata.
Their names slightly vary in the various PurГҘЖ’as. The names given here have been taken from the
BhГҘgavata.
The next verse proceeds to declare the reward of knowing the glory and manifestations
of God described in the preceding five verses.
вҲһГғГҠВў ГҒп¬ӮГ·ГҚГҒГғВў ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘВў Гё в—Ҡв—Ҡ ГҝГҠпЈҝ п¬ӮпЈҝГҒГ»ГҠ ГғГ»п¬ӮГғвҖ”Г”вҖ“
вҖҡГҠпЈҝЛҳГҒп¬ӮвҲ‘В§ГӘВ¬пЈҝЕё ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝЕё ГҝГҲГ–ГҝГғпЈҝ ЕёГҠГІГҠ вҖҡВўвүҘГҠГҝвҖ”Г”H 7H
вҲһГғГҠв—ҠЛңГ” this; ГҒп¬ӮГ·ГҚГҒГғв—ҠЛңГ” supreme divine glory; ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘв—ҠЛңГ” supernatural power; Гё and; в—Ҡв—Ҡ Mine;
ГҝвҖ” who; п¬ӮпЈҝГҒГ»ГҠ knows; ГғГ»п¬ӮГғвҖ” in reality; вҖҡвҖ” he; вҖўГҒп¬ӮвҲ‘В§ГӘВ¬пЈҝЕё ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝЕё through unfaltering devotion;

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
The 
word 
Г«VibhВөutimГӯ, 
with 
the
demonstrative adjective Г«EtГҘmГӯ qualifying it,
covers entities like Buddhi (the faculty of
determining the nature of things) etc., and
great seers and others declared by the Lord
in the preceding three verses as emanating
from Him, as well as the objects, abstract
ideas and gods referred to by Him in such
statements as Г¬I am the sapidity in water
(VII.8)Г® and Г¬I am the Vedic ritual, I am the
sacrifice (IX.16)Г® and so on.
And the word Г«YogamГӯ stands for that
transcendent and marvellous power of God,
the secret of which is not fully known either
to the gods or to the great sages (X.2,3),
which accounts for the fact that, though
Himself both the material and instrumental
cause of entities born of Sattva, Rajas and
Tamas, He always remains apart from them,
justifying the statement Г«neither I exist in
them, nor they in Me (VII.12)Г® by dint of
which, while carrying on the creation,
maintenance and destruction etc., of the whole
universe He governs the entire creation
according to certain definite laws; which makes
Him the supreme Lord of all the worlds, the
disinterested friend of all beings, the enjoyer
of all sacrifices and other noble acts, the
sustainer of all and all-powerful; by which
He holds the entire universe in a fraction of
His being (X.42) and manifests Himself of
His own free will, age after age, in various
forms for various purposes and while doing
all this remains entirely aloof from all actions,
from the entire creation and from all
transformations, such as birth etc., and which
has been spoken of in IX.5 as His wonderful
power of divine Yoga.
In this way the entire universe is GodГӯs
own creation, and everything subsists in a
fraction of His being. Therefore, whatever
object in the world appears as endowed with
power and has some speciality about it, or
better still the whole creation, should be
regarded as a manifestation of, or the same
as, God; while God himself should be
recognized as the creator and destroyer of the
entire universe, the almighty and universal
lord, the sustainer of all, supremely
compassionate, the disinterested friend and
inner controller of all. This is what is meant
by Г«knowing in reality the supreme glory
and supernatural power of God.Гӯ
The word Г«YogenaГӯ accompanied by
the adjective Г«AvikampenaГӯ qualifying it, stands
for that unflinching and exclusive devotion
to God (vide GвҲӮtГҘ XI.54) which is also
named as Г«undivided devotionГӯ in XIII.10,
and as Г«the Yoga of exclusive devotionГӯ in
XIV.26, which has been referred to in VII.1
under the name of Г«YogaГӯ, and the character
of which has been discussed in verses 13, 14
and 34 of the Chapter IX and in verse 9 of
the present chapter. And realizing God through
such devotion is what is meant by getting
united with, or established in God.
He who knows in reality this supreme divine glory and supernatural power of
Mine, gets established in Me through unfaltering Devotion; of this there is no doubt. (7)
Having spoken of God-realization through the practice of unfaltering devotion, the
Lord now discusses in two verses the character of this Yoga of Devotion.
вҖўвҖһВўU вҖҡп¬ӮВёSГҝ В¬ЛқГ·п¬ӮГҠпЈҝ в—ҠГ»ГҠвҖ” вҖҡп¬ӮZ В¬Лқп¬ӮГғВёГғпЈҝГ”вҖ“

* Chapter X *
487
The whole universe has emanated from
God; hence God is both the material and
efficient cause of the entire creation, and
therefore the best of all. To recognize this
fact is to know that He is the source of all
creation.
The wheel of this creation is revolving
through GodГӯs own power of Yoga; it is
through His ruling power that the sun, moon
stars and planets like the earth are regularly
coursing within their respective orbits; it is
under His direction that all living beings are
working out their own destiny after being
born in the higher or lower order of creation
according to their good or evil actions. In this
way to recognize God as the ruler and director
of all is to know that everything in the world
moves becuase of God.
The word Г«BudhГҘВЁГӯ, qualified by the
adjective Г«BhГҘvasamanvitГҘВЁГӯ, stands for those
enlightened devotees who are full of exclusive
love for God, have unflinching faith in Him
and are solely devoted to Him, and who
have full knowledge of His virtues and glory.
Thus recognizing God as the creator,
destroyer and director of the entire universe,
he who constantly remembers and embraces
the Lord through the mind, intellect and
senses, with utmost faith and love, as described
in the following verse, is said to worship
Him according to the spirit of the present
verse.
вҖҡп¬ӮВёв—ҠЛңГ” everything (in the world); В¬Лқп¬ӮГғВёГғпЈҝГ” moves; ГҹГҒГғ thus; в—ҠГ п¬ӮГҠ knowing; Г·ВЎГЈГғпЈҝ (constantly)
worship; в—ҠГҠв—ҠЛңГ” Me (the supreme Deity); вҖҷГҲЕ“ГҠвҖ” the wise; Г·ГҠп¬ӮвҖҡв—ҠГҒГЈп¬ӮГғГҠвҖ” full of devotion.
I am the source of all creation and everything in the world moves because of Me;
knowing thus, the wise, full of devotion, constantly worship Me.                    (8)

With their mind fixed on Me, and their lives surrendered to Me, conversing and
enlightening one another about My glories, My devotees ever remain contented and
take delight in Me.
 (9)
never forget God even for a moment.
And the word Г«MadgataprГҘЖ’ГҘВЁГӯ has been
used with reference to those who live and
breathe solely and entirely for God and whose
senses also function for His sake alone; to
whom even a momentГӯs separation from God
is unbearable, who eat and drink, walk and
move, sleep and keep awake, and carry on
all other activities for His sake alone, and
have no personal interest in those activities.
Loving and faithful devotees of God
fondly indulge in discussing with, and bringing
The word Г«MaccittГҘВЁГӯ refers to those
devotees, who, knowing God as their supreme
lover and greatest friend, nay, their nearest
and dearest one and highest goal, have their
mind exclusively fixed on Him (VIII.14; IX.22);
who have no attraction, attachment or love
for anything else than God; who are ever
engrossed in the thought of His name, virtues,
glory, sports and being; and who, even while
acting in accordance with the scriptural
injunctions and performing ordinary functions
of the body, much less at the time of meditation,

home to, one another the virtues, glory, truth,
sports and mysteries of God on the basis of
their personal experiences and arguing their
case with great skill. This is what is meant by
enlightening one another about the greatness
of God.
Reciting and chanting GodГӯs names,
virtues, glory, stories and character and
popularizing them through sermons and
discourses and offering praises to God etc.,
are all included in speaking of God.
Feeling supreme gratification in doing
all this is what is meant by Г«ever remaining
contented.Гӯ Hearing, reflecting on, chanting
and reciting the names, virtues, glory,
stories and character etc., of God are solely
responsible for the peace, joy and gratification
enjoyed by the devotee who remains ever
contented as mentioned above. Worldly objects
have nothing to do with his joy and
gratification.
Duly hearing, reflecting on and chanting
the names, virtues, glory, stories, character,
truth and mysteries of God, and doing
everything according to His liking, behest
and direction for the sole object of developing
love for Him, such a devotee ever visualizes
Him as actually present before his eyes, and
constantly enjoys His sight and touch, chats
with Him and carries on various other sports
with Him. This is what is meant by ever
taking delight in God.
The Lord now tells us in the next two verses how He rewards the devotees who adore
Him in the above manner.

On those ever united through devotion with Me and worshipping Me with love,
I confer that Yoga of wisdom by which they come to Me.

truth relating to the absolute, formless Divinity
along with the secret of Its glory and greatness,
as also the truth relating to His qualified aspect
with and without form along with His sports,

It is this Buddhiyoga which has been spoken

coupled with VijВӨГҘnaГӯ; and direct realization

ГғпЈҝВ·ГҠв—ҠпЈҝп¬ӮГҠЕёГҲвҲ‘В§ГӘВ¬ГҠГ•Вёв—ҠвҖһUв—ҠГҙГҠГҠЕёВЎВў

ГғпЈҝВ·ГҠв—ҠЛңГ” in order to shower; вҲһп¬ӮГ” Myself; вҖўЕёГҲвҲ‘В§ГӘВ¬ГҠГ•Вёв—ҠЛңГ” My grace on them; вҖўвҖһUв—ҠЛңГ” I; вҖўГҙГҠГҠЕёВЎв—ҠЛңГ” born

In order to shower My grace on them, I, dwelling in their heart, dispel their
darkness born of ignorance by the shining lamp of wisdom.                            (11)
The word Г«JВӨГҘnadвҲӮpenaГӯ, with the
adjective Г«BhГҘsvatГҘГӯ qualifying it, signifies
that transcendent wisdom, free from the faults
of doubt and error etc., which has been
spoken of as Г«BuddhiyogaГӯ or the Yoga of
wisdom in the preceding verse, and as Г«JВӨГҘna
coupled with VijВӨГҘnaГӯ in Chapters VII and
IX, and whereby one is able to know full
well the truth relating to the absolute, formless
Divinity along with Its grandeur and glory,
as well as that relating  to the qualified aspect
of God with and without form along with
His sports, mysteries, glory and greatness
etc., and to dispel the darkness of ignorance
through such wisdom is to tear away from
the heart of the devotee the veil of ignorance
which obstructs the knowledge of Truth.
As a matter of fact, the light of wisdom
dispels the darkness of ignorance and brings
God-realization at the same time. If, however,
some sequence is maintained between the two
processes, it should be understood that the
removal of ignorance takes place earlier and
is immediately followed by God-realization.
By the statement that the Lord Himself
dispels the darkness of ignorance of the
devotee, He seeks to convey that the devotee
need not undertake any other SГҘdhanГҘ for
the purpose. The object is achieved through
the mercy of God alone.
The word Г«TamaВЁГӯ qualified by the
adjective Г«AjВӨГҘnajamГӯ, stands for the veiling
power of ignorance that has existed from time
without beginning, and due to which man fails
to realize the virtues, glory and character of
God. By the statement Г¬dwelling in the heart I
dispel their ignoranceГ® the Lord reveals the
glory of devotion and exculpates Himself from
the charge of partiality. The Lord thereby seeks
to convey that He constantly resides in the
heart of all as their Inner Controller, and yet
people do not feel His presence within their
heart; that is why He fails to dispel the ignorance
of their heart. His loving devotees, however,
as indicated in the preceding verse, constantly
see Him as if actually present in their heart; it
is therefore that He easily dispels the darkness
of their ignorance.
Г¬O all-virtuous Lord ! without Thee I seek neither abode in the uppermost heaven nor the position
of BrahmГҘ (the Creator), neither sovereignty over the entire globe nor dominion over the nether worlds,
neither the achievements of Yoga nor even lasting freedom from rebirth.Г®

490
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
By the first half of the twelfth verse Arjuna
intends to say that the absolute known as the
Г«transcendent EternalГӯ is no other than вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a; that His supreme Abode too, being
the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and
Bliss, divine in character and hence identical
with God, is the same as вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a; and that
His names, virtues, glory, stories and character,
when heard, reflected on and chanted, are
supremely purifying in every way.
The Lord discussed in Chapter VII the subject which He introduced in the very opening
verse of that chapter as leading to a knowledge of His integral Self and which He undertook
in the next verse to expound under the name of Г«JВӨГҘna, and VijВӨГҘnaГӯ, or the knowledge of
the NirguЖ’a and SaguЖ’a aspect of God. Thereafter in Chapter VIII, He threw further light
on the same subject even while answering the seven questions of Arjuna. But since His
method of exposition was different in that chapter, He undertook once more at the beginning
of Chapter IX to discuss the subject of JВӨГҘna and VijВӨГҘna and expounded it at full length
in all its details. Again, in order to elucidate the subject further and in a different language,
the Lord undertook in the opening verse of Chapter X to discuss the subject again; and
having devoted the next five verses to a description of His Yogic power and glories, He
declared in the seventh that knowledge of the latter leads to His own realization through
unfaltering devotion. Thereafter, describing in the eighth and the ninth the mental attitude
and conduct of those engaged in worshipping Him through the discipline of Bhakti, the Lord
wound up the topic by pointing out in the tenth and eleventh the fruit of such worship, viz.,
the attainment of the Yoga of wisdom, which dispels the darkenss of ignorance and leads
to God-realization, thereupon realizing that knowledge of the Yogic power and glories of
God are exceedingly helpful in God-realization, Arjuna now devotes the next seven verses
to praises of the Lord, praying in the end that the Lord may be pleased to describe in detail
His own Yogic power and glories.
вҖўВЎГҲВёЕё В©Uп¬ӮГҠГё
В¬вҒ„ВўU вҖҷЛқrГҠГ” В¬вҒ„ВўU Е“ГҠв—Ҡ В¬ГҒп¬ӮГІГҠВў В¬вҒ„Uв—ҠВў Г·п¬ӮГҠЕёГ”ЛңГ”вҖ“
В¬ГҲLВ§В·Вў вүҘГҠГҠEГғВў ГҒЕ’вҲҡГҝв—ҠГҠГҒЕ’Е’пЈҝп¬Ӯв—ҠВЎВў ГҒп¬ӮГ·ГҲв—ҠЛңГ”H 12H
вҖўГҠвҖһГҲUSГ п¬ӮГҠв—ҠГҺВ·ГҝвҖ” вҖҡп¬ӮпЈҝВё Е’пЈҝп¬ӮГҢВ·ЕёГҠВёвҒ„UЕ’SГғГ•ГҠГ”вҖ“

В¬вҒ„Uв—ҠЛңГ” вҖҷЛқrГҠГ” the transcendent Eternal; В¬вҒ„Uв—ҠЛңГ” Е“ГҠв—ҠГ” the supreme abode; В¬ГҒп¬ӮГІГҠв—ҠЛңГ” В¬вҒ„Uв—Ҡв—ҠЛңГ” (and) the
greatest purifier; Г·п¬ӮГҠЕёЛңГ” You; В¬ГҲLВ§В·в—ҠЛңГ” вүҘГҠГҠEГғв—ҠЛңГ” ГҒЕ’вҲҡГҝв—ҠЛңГ” the eternal divine PuruВ¶a; вҖўГҠГҒЕ’Е’пЈҝп¬Ӯв—ҠЛңГ” the primal

Е’пЈҝп¬ӮГҢВ·вҖ” ЕёГҠвҒ„UЕ’вҖ” the celestial sage NГҘrada; ГғГ•ГҠ even so; вҖўГҒвҖҡГғвҖ” Е’пЈҝп¬ӮвҲӮUвҖ” the sages Asita and

в—ҠпЈҝ to Me.
Arjuna said: You are the transcendent Eternal, the supreme Abode and the greatest
purifier; all the seers speak of You as the eternal divine PuruВ¶a, the primal Deity,
unborn and all-pervading. Likewise speak the celestial sage NГҘrada, the sages Asita
and Devala and the great sage VyГҘsa; and Yourself too proclaim this to me.    (12,13)

The following are the marks of a DevarВ¶i

Г¬Seers living in the celestial regions
should be known as the blessed DevarВ¶is.

well who are distinguished by their knowledge
of the past, present and future and strict

BrГҘhmaЖ’as, and members of the royal class

Г¬The root Г«В°RВ¶Гӯ is used in the sense of motion, (Knowledge), hearing truth and austerity. BrahmГҘ,

Describing the glory of BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a to Duryodhana, the patriarch BhвҲӮВ¶ma, the great

Г¬BhagavГҘn VГҘsudeva (the son of Vasudeva) is the object of worship of all gods and the best of
all beings. He is virtue personified and the knower of the secret of Dharma, the bestower of boons and
the satisfier of all desires; He is the doer, the deed and the Lord Himself. The past, present and future,
the morning and evening twilights, the four quarters, the heaven and all laws are the creations of this
very Lord. It is this high-souled and imperishable Lord who created the В°RВ¶is, austerity and the PrajГҘpatis,
who have brought forth the entire creation. He is the Creator of SaвҲҶkarВ¶aЖ’a himself, the eldest of all creation.
вҖәeВ¶a (the serpent-god), popularly known as Г«AnantaГӯ (the eternal being), who carries the weight of the
entire globe including the mountains on his head, also emanated from Him. It is He who takes the form
of the divine Boar, the Man-Lion, and the divine Dwarf; He is the father and mother of all. There is
none superior to Him. Г«KeвҖ№avaГӯ as He is called, He is the embodiment of supreme lustre and the grandfather
of all beings; the seers call Him HГҰВ¶ikeвҖ№a. He is the teacher, the forefather and the preceptor. He who
wins the favour of вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a attains the imperishable abode. He who, when confronted with danger, seeks
refuge in вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and offers praises to Him, attains supreme happiness.Г®

492
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
Г¬The two sons of Dharma, Nara and
NГҘrГҘyaЖ’a, KratuГӯs sons, collectively known
as the VГҘlakhilyas, Kardama, son of Pulaha,
Parvata, NГҘrada and the two sons of KaвҖ№yapa,
Asita and Vatsara, because they can exercise
control even over the celestials, are called
DevarВ¶is.Г®
DevarВ¶i NГҘrada, Asita, Devala and VyГҘsa
Гіall these great В°RВ¶is know of the real truth
about God, and are great lovers and devotees
of God, and possessed of supreme wisdom.*
They are regarded as highly respected
and truthful sages of their time; that is why
their names have been specially mentioned in
*
Though there have been more than one person bearing the name of NГҘrada, history records no
two DevarВ¶is of this name. The scriptures speak of him as the Г«mindГӯ of God. Possessing supreme knowledge
of the divine Truth and supreme love for God, he is a celibate of the order known as ВӘUrdhvaretГҘs (so-
called because their generative fluid or creative energy has attained an upward flow). He is the foremost
teacher of Bhakti or Devotion. The world owes a great deal to him. It was he who inspired great devotees
like PrahlГҘda, Dhruva and AmbarвҲӮВ¶a, and showed them the way to Bhakti; and the world further vowes
to him two incomparable gems of Indian literature, viz., вҖәrвҲӮmad-BhГҘgavata and the RГҘmГҘyaЖ’a of VГҘlmвҲӮki.
It was he who imparted instruction even to a man of great spiritual wisdom like вҖәrвҲӮ вҖәukadeva.
In his previous incarnation, NГҘrada was the son of a maid-servant who used to scour the unclean
utensils of great sages. When he was only five years of age, His mother died of an accident. Thus freed
from all worldly ties, he left all human habitation and retired into the woods, where he sat under the
foot of a big tree and began to meditate on God. In the course of this meditation, his thoughts got focussed
on one point and the Lord revealed Himself in his heart. But having blessed him with a glimpse of His
soul-enchanting beauty for a short while, He suddenly disappeared from his view. Feeling greatly disturbed
at His sudden disappearance, he concentrated his mind once more and began to meditate on the Lord,
but failed to obtain His vision any more. Meanwhile he heard an ethereal voice say, Г¬O child, you will
not be blessed with My vision any more in your present incarnation. Having cast off this mortal coil
you will attain Me as one of My own associates.Г® These words of the Lord brought him great consolation,
and he moved about in the world, free from attachment, awaiting the last moment. At the appointed hour
he left physical body, and in the next Kalpa he assumed a divine form and was reborn from the mind
of BrahmГҘ. Since then, observing a vow of unbroken celibacy, he has been singing the glories of the
Lord to the accompaniment of his lute (BhГҘgavata I.vi).
Chapter V of MahГҘbhГҘrata, SabhГҘ-Parva, contains the following account of DevarВ¶i NГҘrada:Гі
Г¬DevarВ¶i NГҘrada knows the real meaning of the Vedas and UpaniВ¶ads, is adored by the celestials,
has specialized in the branch of literature known as the ItihГҘsas and PurГҘЖ’as, possesses knowledge of
the events of the previous Kalpas, knows the essence of virtue and justice and is the best scholar of вҖәikВ¶ГҘ
(phonetics), Kalpa (the science of ritual), VyГҘkaraЖ’a (grammar), Г…yurveda (the science of medicine) and
other branches. Nay, he is an expert in reconciling scriptural injunctions of a contradictory nature, a
powerful orator, well-versed in politics, possessed of great intelligence, memory and wisdom, a poet, clever
in distinguishing good from evil, capable of ascertaining the reality of things by employing the various
methods of reasoning, proficient in discovering the beauties and fallacies of syllogistic reasoning and
capable of meeting the objections of scholars like BГҰhaspati. He knows in reality the truth about Dharma
(religious merit), Artha (worldly riches), KГҘma (enjoyment) and MokВ¶a (liberation), is capable of directly
perceiving by his Yogic power every incident taking place in this world as well as in the higher and
lower worlds, clever in distinguishing the paths of Knowledge and Action, expert in giving lessons to
the gods and demons on dispassion, a master statesman proficient in the Knowledge of war and peace,
an adept in determining what should be done and what should not be done, incomparable in the
employment of the six expedients of royal policy, well-versed in all the scriptures, and well-versed in
the science of warfare, a master of music, a great devotee of God, a repository of all learning and virtues,
the very prop of morality and the universal friend, and has access everywhere.Г® The UpaniВ¶ads, PurГҘЖ’as
and ItihГҘsas are replete with his sacred stories.

The sages Asita and Devala are related as father and son. The KВөurma-PurГҘЖ’a contains the following
reference to them:Гі
вҲһГғГҠЕёГҲГ В¬ГҠl  В¬ГҲГІГҠГҠВўSГғГҲ 
В¬ЛқВЎГҠвҖҡГЈГғГҠЕёвҲ‘В§ГҠвҒ„UГЎГҠГҠГғЛңГ” вҖ“ вҲ‘В§вүҘГҝВ¬вҖ” В¬ГҲГІГҠвҲ‘В§ГҠв—ҠSГғГҲ ГёГёГҠвҒ„U вҖҡГҲв—ҠвҖһUГ»ГҠВ¬вҖ”H

вҖҡГҲГғГҠГҒп¬Ӯв—ҠГҠГ’ вҖ“ п¬ӮГ вҖҡвҒ„UpГҠГҒвҖҡГғpГ’п¬Ӯ

вҖўГҒвҖҡГғSГҝГ’вҲ‘В§В¬ГЎГҠГҠВёГҝГҠВў 
вҖҷЛқГҒrГҠГ”DГ”UвҖ” 
вҖҡв—ҠВ¬lГғ вҖ“ ЕёГҠв—ҠAГҠ п¬ӮГ’ Е’пЈҝп¬ӮвҲӮUвҖ” В¬ГҲГІГҠГҠпЈҝ ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘГҠГёГҠГҝГҠпЈҝВё в—ҠвҖһUГҠГғВ¬ГҠвҖ”H
(KВөurma-PurГҘЖ’a XIX.1,2,5)
Г¬Having begotten these sons with the object of multiplying creation, the sage KaвҖ№yapa began once
more to practise hard penance with the intention of getting more sons. As the result of his severe penance
he got two more sons. Vatsara and Asita by name. Both of them possessed divine knowledge and were
teachers of Veda. Asita begot through his wife, EkaparЖ’ГҘ, a son, Devala by name, who was well-versed
in the Vedas, a teacher of Yoga and a great ascetic.Г®
Asita and Devala are both seers of the В°Rgveda. В°RВ¶i Devala attained perfection through the worship
of Lord вҖәiva. Both these seers are ancient and highly accomplished В°RВ¶is. PratyВөuВ¶a, one of the eight Vasus,
also had son who was known as RВ¶i Devala (HarivaвүҲвҖ№a, III.44).
*
*
*
The sage VedavyГҘsa is believed to be a part manifestation of God. He was born in an Island (DvвҲӮpa),
which got him the title of Г«DvaipГҘyanaГӯ. On account of his dark complexion he was known as Г«KГҰВ¶Ж’a
DvaipГҘyanaГӯ; and because he arranged the Vedas, he was called Г«VedavyГҘsaГӯ. He is the son of the great
sage ParГҘвҖ№ara. His motherГӯs name was SatyavatвҲӮ. As soon as he was born he retired into the woods with
the intention of practising austerities. He has full knowledge of the reality about God and is a peerless
poet. He is a boundless and fathomless ocean of knowledge, the farthest limit of erudition and the highest
perfection of poetry. The unfolding of VyГҘsaГӯs heart and speech constitute the very lustre and foundation
of worldГӯs knowledge.
The BrahmasВөutras were written by BhagavГҘn VyГҘsa himself. He was, again, the author of such a
wonderful scripture as the MahГҘbhГҘrata. The eighteen principal PurГҘЖ’as and many more subsidiary PurГҘЖ’as
were compiled by him. The history of India bears testimony to this fact. It is from the gift of wisdom
bequeathed by VyГҘsa that the whole world is seeking light today.
Every DvГҘpara has its own VyГҘsa for arranging the Vedas. The present VyГҘsa, KГҰВ¶Ж’a DvaipГҘyana,
son of ParГҘвҖ№ara is the 28th VedavyГҘsa of the current or Vaivasvata Manvantara. He taught В°Rgveda to
his principal disciple Paila, Yajurveda to VaiвҖ№ampГҘyana, SГҘmaveda to Jaimini and Atharvaveda to Sumantu,
and further taught the ItihГҘsas and PurГҘЖ’as to the great and highly talented sage RomaharВ¶aЖ’a, who was
a SВөuta (charioteer) by birth.
*  DevarВ¶i NГҘrada said, Г¬BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a is the Creator of all the worlds, omniscient and the
supreme Lord of all deities and SГҘdhyas.Г®
The sage MГҘrkaЖ’Е’eya said, Г¬вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a is the sacrifice of sacrifices and the penance of penances,
and embodies in Himself the past, present and future.Г®
BhГҰgu said, Г¬He is the god of gods and the most ancient ViВ¶Ж’u.Г®
VyГҘsa said, Г¬He confers Indrahood on Indra, and is the Supreme Deity of all deities.Г®
AвҲҶgirГҘ said, Г¬He is the Creator of all beings.Г®

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
The monosyllable word Г«KaГӯ represents
BrahmГҘ (the Creator), Г«AГӯ signifies ViВ¶Ж’u
(the Protector) and Г«вҲ«вҖ№aГӯ is a synonym of вҖәiva
(Destroyer of the universe). KeвҖ№ava, therefore,
is the Deity who represents all these three
aspects of God. Addressing вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a as
Г«KeвҖ№avaГӯ in this verse, Arjuna, therefore,
intends to convey that unquestionably He is
the Supreme Lord responsible for the creation,
sustenance and destruction of the whole
universe.
The pronouns Г«EtatГӯ and Г«YatГӯ, used in
this verse, point to all that has been stated by
the Lord from the beginning of Chapter VII
to verse 11 of the present Chapter regarding
his own virtues, glory, character, greatness
mystery and supernatural powers etc., and
which constitutes a direct admission on His
part of His divinity. And recognizing вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a
as God Himself, the integral Brahma, the
вҖҡп¬ӮВёв—ҠпЈҝГғЕ’ГҺГғВў в—ҠГЈГҝпЈҝ ГҝГЈв—ҠГҠВў п¬ӮЕ’ГҒвҖҡ вҲ‘пЈҝВ§вүҘГҠп¬ӮГ”вҖ“

aware of Your manifestations through sport (lвҲӮlГҘ).                                           (14)
SanatkumГҘra and others said, Г¬His head pervades the heavens and His arms cover the earth; all the
three worlds are contained in His belly. He is the eternal PuruВ¶a. The SГҘdhaka can know Him only after
attaining purification of the heart. He is considered to be the Best even among seers who are sated through
Self-realization; and He is the ultimate resort even of the lofty-minded royal sages, who never turn their
back on the field of battle (MahГҘ., BhвҲӮВ¶ma., Cap. 68).
In Chapter XII of the MahГҘbhГҘrata, Vana-Parva, the great devotee among women, DraupadвҲӮ, quotes
the following words of a number of sages:Гі
The sages Asita and Devala said, Г¬вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a Himself is the PrajГҘpati (the Lord of creation) during
the primal creation of beings, and is the sole creator of all the worlds.Г®
ParaвҖ№urГҘma said, Г¬He is ViВ¶Ж’u Himself; none can vanquish Him. He is the sacrifice, the sacrificer
and the deity worshipped through sacrifice.Г®
NГҘrada said, Г¬He is the supreme Lord of all deities known as the SГҘdhyas, as well as of all
blessings.Г®
Г¬Just as a child plays at will with its dolls, so does вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a sport in the company of BrahmГҘ,
вҖәiva, Indra and the other gods.Г®
Besides the above, BhagavГҘn VyГҘsa says in the MahГҘbhГҘrata:Гі
Г¬There is a sacred town in SaurГҘВ¶tГҰa (the modern Kathiawad), named DvГҘrakГҘ, wherein resides
BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, the most ancient and supreme PuruВ¶a.  He is SanГҘtana Dharma personified. Brahmans
well-versed in the Vedas and men who have realized the Self declare high-souled вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a to be SanГҘtana
Dharma itself. He is the most sacred of all sacred objects, the holiest of the holy, and the most auspicious
of all auspicious things. BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, possessed of lotus-like eyes, is the eternal god of all the
gods in the three worlds. He is the imperishable soul and perishable matter, the conscious self, the supreme
Lord and one endowed with a form which is beyond human conception.Г® (MahГҘ., Vana., 8.24 to 27).
In вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata DevarВ¶i NГҘrada, addressing the pious king YudhiВ¶Вўhira, says, Г¬O king, of all
men you are indeed highly blessed, inasmuch as sages, who lend sanctity to the worlds, frequently visit
your palaces, and the supreme Brahma Himself lives here in the guise of a human being. Indeed, вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a
is the supreme Brahma whom sages seek to realize as the bliss of absolute repose. He is your beloved
friend, your maternal cousin, the object of your worship, your Guide and teacher. Tell me, then, who can
compare with you in the matter of good luck?Г® (вҖәrвҲӮmad BhГҘgavata VII.xv.75-76).

В©UГ В¬ГҒГ»ГҠВў В¬ЛқвҲӮUГҝВў ГёГ’п¬Ӯ Г·ГҚГғГҠЕёГҠв—ҠГҠВӘГҒГғВў ВӘГҒГғв—ҠЛңГ”вҖ“

Г¬He who is aware of the creation and
dissolution of the universe, of the appearance
and disappearance of beings, as well as of
VidyГҘ (wisdom) and AvidyГҘ (nescience),
should be designated as Г«BhagavГҘnГӯ.Г®
By addressing вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a as Г«BhagavГҘnГӯ,
Arjuna means to say that he is God Himself,
the repository of all glory and omniscient.
The word Г«VyaktimГӯ denotes all the
various forms assumed by God by way of
sport from time to time for the creation,
maintenance and destruction of the universe,
for the rehabilitation of virtue, for redeeming
the devotees by blessing them with His sight,
for the protection of gods and destruction of
the demons, and for various other reasons.
By saying that the secret of His manifestation
in various forms is known neither to the
demons nor to the gods, Arjuna intends to
convey that even though the demons are
endowed with the capacity to assume various
forms by dint of their MГҘyГҘ or conjuring
power and the gods are capable of perceiving
supersensuous 
objects, 
yet 
they 
are
unable to perceive the divine character of His
various forms, or to comprehend His divine
power, and art of assuming those forms, the
occasion for such manifestation and the
inwardness of His sports. Much less can
an ordinary mortal expect to know these
things.

* Chapter X *
497
He of whom all men seek their desired
objects is called JanГҘrdana (ВЎЕёГ’вҖ” вҖўlВёГғпЈҝ). By
addressing вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a as Г«JanГҘrdanaГӯ, Arjuna
indicates that all men approach Him for the
attainment of their desired objects, and also
that He is fully capable of granting all their
prayers. Arjuna thereby hopes that his prayer
too will be granted by the Lord.
Г«YogaГӯ is that divine potency of God by
which He manifests Himself in the form of
this universe and thereby reproduces Himself
in various forms; and His manifestation in
those forms is called His Г«VibhВөutiГӯ or glory.
Both these words have already occurred in
verse 7 above, and their meaning has been
fully explained in our commentary on the
same. Realization of God through unfaltering
devotion has been mentioned in that verse as
the reward of knowing His Yoga and VibhВөuti
in reality. Therefore, with a view to knowing
full well the secret of His Yoga and VibhВөuti,
Arjuna requests the Lord to describe them in
detail again and again.
The words of вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a appear very sweet
to Arjuna; he finds such a stream of joy flowing
from them that his mind never gets wearied
of drinking of it. The more he drinks of that
nectar, the more his thirst for the same grows.
He feels as if he should go on drinking that
nectar for ever. Therefore, he prays that вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a should go on pouring that divine nectar
into his ever thirsty ears. He should not feel
deterred by the thought that such and such a
thing has already been said or that the subject
has been amply thrashed out so that there is
no need to dilate on it any more.
KГҰВ¶Ж’a, tell me once more in detail of Your power of Yoga and Your glory; for
I know no satiety in hearing Your nectar-like words.                                  (18)
In response to this prayer of Arjuna for a detailed and full description of His power
of Yoga and glory, the Lord first points out the limitless range of His magnitude and then
undertakes to describe only His outstanding glories.

there is no limit to My magnitude, indeed.                                                      (19)
denote only such objects or beings which

As promised in the preceding verse the Lord now first of all describes His divine glories
in verses 20 to 39:Гі
вҖўвҖһUв—ҠГҠГ в—ҠГҠ ВӘГҲВ«UГҠвҲ‘пЈҝВ§вүҘГҠ вҖҡп¬ӮВёГ·ГҚГғГҠвүҘГҠГҝГҒSГ•ГғвҖ”вҖ“

вҖўвҖһUв—ҠЛңГ” I; вҖўГҠГ в—ҠГҠ the (universal) self; ВӘГҲВ«UГҠвҲ‘пЈҝВ§вүҘГҠU O Arjuna (lit., a conqueror of sleep);
