Endowed with a pure intellect and partaking of a light, SГҘttvika and regulated
diet, living in a lonely and undefiled place, having rejected sound and other objects of
sense, having controlled the mind, speech and body by restraining the mind and senses
through firmness of a SГҘttvika type, taking a resolute stand on dispassion, after having
completely got rid of attraction and aversion and remaining ever devoted to the Yoga
of meditation, having given up egotism, violence, arrogance, lust, anger and luxuries,
devoid of the feeling of meum and tranquil of heart,Гіsuch a man becomes qualified
for oneness with Brahma, who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss.

giving up Г«ParigrahaГӯ primarily means actually

Broadly speaking, however, even he who

Giving up all these and suspending the

Г«NirmamaВЁГӯ is he who completely
renounces the feeling of Г«mineГӯ in respect of
the body including the mind and senses, in
respect of all living beings, actions and
enjoyments and even with regard to oneГӯs
birth, race, nationality, grade in society and
stage in lifeГіwho ceases to distinguish an
object, activity or living being as his own or
alien.
As a result of the above practices, the
mind of the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ is no longer distracted
by worldly thoughts and is ever pervaded by
an unruffled calm and pure, SГҘttvika joy.
The quietist who is possessed of such a
tranquil mind is called Г«вҖәГҘntaВЁГӯ.
A man who is endowed with all these
accomplishments not only becomes eligible
for oneness with Brahma but immediately
becomes one with the Absolute. That is to
say, the distinction between the soul and the
Oversoul no longer exists in his eyes and the
realization that he is no other than Brahma,Гі
who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss
solidified,Гіgets firmly rooted in him. In that
state he beholds his own self as projected in
the whole universe and the whole universe
as conceived in his own being (VI.29).
Having thus discussed the true nature of SaвүҲnyГҘsa or SГҘвҲҶkhyayoga (the path of
Knowledge) in all its details, the Lord now points out the marks of the YogвҲӮ who has become
one with Brahma through the practice of that discipline, and tells us how he reaches the
culmination of JВӨГҘnayoga, termed as ParГҘ Bhakti.

verse 27 of Chapter VI also speak of the

Г«PrasannГҘtmГҘГӯ is he whose mind is sinless,
free from impurities and unruffled, and ever
remains merged in pure delight. The use of
this adjective is intended to convey that
everything other than Brahma having ceased
to exist in the eyes of the man who has attained
oneness with Brahma, his mind is ever calm
and never gets excited on any account.
The words Г«Na вҖ№ocati na kГҘвҲҶkВ¶atiГӯ
represent the distinguishing marks of the YogвҲӮ
who has attained oneness with Brahma. The
idea is that having come to recognize Brahma
in everything such a YogвҲӮ no longer
differentiates any object from himself nor
regards anything as attractive nor entertains
the feeling of Г«mineГӯ with regard to anything.
That is why it makes no difference to him
when anyone gets united with or disjoined
from the body etc. Hence on no account and
in no circumstance whatsoever does he give
way to grief or anxiety in the least degree.
And he gets sated inasmuch as he ceases to
veiw anything as other than Brahma; hence
he craves for nothing.
The adjectival phrase Г«SarveВ¶u bhВөuteВ¶u
samaВЁГӯ is intended to show his feeling of
non-differentiation towards all beings. The
idea is that the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ referred to above
does not view any living being as other than
himself; hence he ceases to differentiate one
from another. He comes to develop the feeling
of oneness towards all. This very idea has
been brought out in verse 29 of Chapter VI
by the words Г«Sarvatra samadarвҖ№anaВЁГӯ.
The compound word Г«MadbhaktimГӯ,
qualified by the adjective Г«ParГҘmГӯ, denotes
that which is the fruit of JВӨГҘnayoga and
which is also termed as the culmination of
JВӨГҘna and the realization of Truth; for having
revealed the true nature of God it unites one
with Him.
Declaring thus that the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ who has become one with Brahma attains Г«ParГҘ
BhaktiГӯ, the Lord now points out its reward.

Through that supreme devotion he comes to know Me in reality, what and who
I am; and thereby knowing Me truly, he forthwith merges into My being.

The word Г«BhaktyГҘГӯ in this verse refers
to the same realization of Truth which has
been spoken of as Г«ParГҘm madbhaktimГӯ in
the preceding verse and as the culmination
of knowledge in verse 50. This is the fruit
of JВӨГҘnayoga, Bhaktiyoga, Karmayoga,
DhyГҘnayoga and all other disciplines; it is
through this that all SГҘdhakas attain true
knowledge of God and realize God Himself.
It is in order to point out the identity of the fruit
of all disciplines that the word Г«BhaktyГҘГӯ has
been used in this context dealing with JВӨГҘnayoga.
Synchronously with the attainment of this
ParГҘ Bhakti, which is only another name for
the realization of Truth, the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ,
referred to above, comes to know the real
character of God with the help of that
realization. The nature of His absolute, formless
aspect, His qualified formless aspect as well
as of His qualified aspect with form, and how

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
even though it follows the verb Г«JВӨГҘtvГҘГӯ,
does not imply sequence of time. The use of
the verb Г«JВӨГҘtvГҘ (having known) no doubt
gives one the impression that the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ
enters into the being of God some time after
realizing the truth about Him. But it is in
order to remove this impression that the Lord
uses the word Г«TadanantaramГӯ. The idea is
that there is no interval or lapse of time
between the knowledge of the true nature of
God and His own realization; true knowledge
of His essential character and entering into
His beingГіboth come together. God, being
the Self of all, is not really something
unattained by any; therefore, He is realized
as soon as His reality is known. Hence it is
in order to bring out this idea that the word
Г«TadanantaramГӯ, has been taken to mean
Г«immediatelyГӯ or Г«at onceГӯ. For the verb Г«JВӨГҘtvГҘГӯ
was enough to denote sequence of time; the
use of the word Г«TadanantaramГӯ would,
therefore, become superfluous or redundant
if it were taken in that sense.
he emerges with a form from the formless
state and how He withdraws His form and
reverts to the formless stateГіall this becomes
known to him. That is why diversity of any
kind ceases to exist in his eyes. It is in order
to reveal the identity of God in His qualified
aspect with the absolute, formless Brahma,
attained through the practice of JВӨГҘnayoga or
the discipline of Knowledge, that the Lord
has used the personal pronoun Г«MГҘmГӯ.
The indeclinable Г«TataВЁГӯ implies cause.
God-realization takes place as soon as the
essential character or true nature of God is
knownГіthere is no interval or lapse of time
between the two events; hence the word has
not been interpreted to mean Г«thereafterГӯ. The
word Г«TataВЁГӯ refers to a cause which forms
the subject of discussion; and it was also
necessary to repeat the cause alongwith the
verb Г«JВӨГҘtvГҘГӯ; hence Г«TataВЁГӯ should be taken
to refer to the Г«ParГҘ BhaktiГӯ mentioned in the
first half of the verse.
The compound adverb Г«TadanantaramГӯ,
Having thus expounded severally the truth relating to TyГҘga or Karmayoga on the one
hand and that of SaвүҲnyГҘsa or SГҘвҲҶkhyayoga on the other in response to the enquiry of
Arjuna, the Lord concluded here the discussion of SГҘвҲҶkhyayoga. He, however, did not tell
him which of the two disciplines should be followed by him. Therefore, with a view to
inducing him to adopt Karmayoga with special emphasis on Bhakti, the Lord now glorifies
Karmayoga dominated by Bhakti.

The KarmayogвҲӮ, however, who depends on Me, attains by My grace the eternal,
imperishable state, though performing all actions.

The 
compound 
adjective 
Г«Mad-
vyapГҘвҖ№rayaВЁГӯ in this verse refers to the man
who practises Karmayoga with special
emphasis on Bhakti. Such a KarmayogвҲӮ gives
up dependence on all actions and their fruit
in the shape of all enjoyments and takes
refuge in God alone. Having dedicated his
body, including the mind and senses, and all
its activities as well as their fruit to God, he
no longer treats them as his own, withdraws
his attachment from them and ceases to hanker
for anything, and remains exclusively devoted

=== PAGE 764 ===

* Chapter XVIII *
763
to Him. He looks upon God as his supreme
goal, the highest object of his love, his greatest
well-wisher, chief supporter and sole refuge,
and remains cheerful under all circumstances
taking them to be a dispensation of God.
That is to say, he never rejoices nor grieves
over the appearance or disappearance of any
worldly object or over any event and ever
depends on God. Whatever he does is according
to His behest, for His pleasure, and under
His guidance and inspiration regarding himself
as a mere instrument and working just as he
is made to work, and views himself as entirely
subject to His control. All this is covered by
the adjective Г«MadvyapГҘвҖ№rayaВЁГӯ.
The compound word Г«SarvakarmГҘЖ’iГӯ
covers all duties that have been prescribed in
the вҖәГҘstras with due regard to the VarЖ’a and
Г…вҖ№rama of an individual, that have been
referred to above by the terms Г«Niyatam
KarmaГӯ and Г«SvabhГҘvajam KarmaГӯ and which
conform to the commandments and directions
of God.
The use of the particle Г«ApiГӯ is intended
to glorify the man who practises Karmayoga
with special emphasis on Bhakti, and to
show that Karmayoga is easy to practise.
The idea is that while the SГҘвҲҶkhyayogвҲӮ realizes
God by renouncing all luxuries and enjoyments
and practising constant meditation on God in
a lonely place, the KarmayogвҲӮ who has taken
refuge in God realizes the same God even
while performing all duties appropriate to his
VarЖ’a or grade in society and Г…вҖ№rama or
stage in life. There is no disparity whatsoever
in the fruit of the two disciplines.
The substantive Г«PadamГӯ qualified by the
adjectives Г«вҖәГҘвҖ№vatamГӯ and Г«AvyayamГӯ, stands
for the almighty and all-supporting God, who
has existed from eternity and remains for ever,
who never ceases to be, and who is Truth,
Consciousness and Bliss solidified. He has
been called Г«PadamГӯ in order to show that He
alone is the supreme goal. The same principle
which has been termed as Г«SaвүҲsiddhiГӯ in verse
45 above, as Г«SiddhiГӯ in the next verse and as
Г«MГҘmГӯ or God in verse 55 above has been
referred to in the present verse as the eternal,
imperishable state. The idea is that the same
truth has been called by different names.
Impressed and pleased with the sentiment of
the man practising Karmayoga with special
emphasis on Bhakti, the Lord confers on him
of His own accord the Yoga of wisdom known
as ParГҘ Bhakti (X.10); realizing the true nature
of God through that Buddhiyoga the devotee
becomes one with or gets merged in God,
who is Truth, Knowledge and Bliss solidified.
This is what is meant by the latter half of this
verse.
Extolling thus the man who practises Karmayoga with the element of Bhakti preponderating
in it, the Lord now commands Arjuna to become such a KarmayogвҲӮ.

The man who resigns all his actions to
God feels that his mind, senses and body, the
activities performed by them and everything
in the world belong to Him, and accordingly

He who is exclusively devoted to God
(Г«MatparaВЁГӯ) looks upon Him as his supreme
goal, highest asylum, greatest wellwisher,
dearest object of love and chief support. He is
ever contented with His dispensation and
remains devoted to practices of God-realization.
Even so Г«MaccittaВЁГӯ is he who has his
mind and intellect irrevocably fastened on God.
Conceiving not the least attachment for anyone
other than God, he remains solely engaged in
thinking of God in terms of exclusive love.
Even a momentГӯs gap in the thought of God
becomes unbearable to him. He continues to

while he is sitting or standing, walking or
moving from one place to another, eating or

ceases to look upon any of these as his own,

Viewing success and failure, joy and

that whatever happens in the world is controlled

no longer entertains the discriminative feelings

With your mind thus given to Me, you shall, by My grace, tide over all difficulties.

Having resigned all his actions to God
in the manner suggested above, and constantly
fixing his mind on Him, Arjuna would be
required to do nothing more. By the LordГӯs
grace all his sorrows in this world as well as
in the next would be easily obviated. Rid of
all vices and evil propensities, he would be
exempted for ever from the great ordeal in
the shape of birth and death and would attain
God, who is eternal Bliss solidified. This is
what the Lord seeks to convey in the first
half of this verse.
Arjuna was a devotee and beloved friend
of the Lord; he was, therefore, sure to obey
Him. The Lord, however, warned Arjuna that
just as obedience to Him was highly beneficial,
disobedience to Him involved great risk;
therefore, if from self-conceit Arjuna did not
obey the Lord and acted according
to his own will he was doomed. In that case he

that the Lord has already met this objection by

That he should not listen to the Lord, and should

The Lord has on a previous occasion

The word Г«PrakГҰtiГӯ in this verse denotes
the sum-total of tendencies formed as a result
of actions performed through a number of
lives, and manifesting themselves in the current
life of an individual in the form of temperament
or disposition; it is also called the nature of
an individual. It is this nature that determines
the birth of a soul in a community endowed
with an aptitude for a particular type of
actions; and it is under the influence of this
nature that different individuals are inclined
towards diverse types of actions. The words
Г«PrakГҰtiВЁ twГҘm niyokВ¶yatiГӯ are, therefore,

nature, which has caused his birth in the
Commanded by the Lord on a previous
occasion to fight (II.3), Arjuna openly said,
Г¬I will not fightГ® (II.9). The first half of the
present verse has reference to that refusal on
the part of Arjuna. The idea is that the
resolve of Arjuna not to fight was nothing
but a display of vanity on his part: he was
not free to desist from war. It was, therefore,
most undesirable that Arjuna should thus
allow himself to be possessed by egotism
born of ignorance, and should accordingly
regard himself as wise, competent and free
and take upon himself on the strength of
such egotism the responsibility of accomplishing
a particular act in a particular way and make
up his mind to desist from a particular course
of action. This is what the Lord seeks to
impress upon Arjuna in the first half of this
verse.

to the process laid down in verse 57 above,
Arjuna would be free from the bondage of
Karma and realize the Lord; or else being

mundane existence in the form of birth and
death. This is what the Lord seeks to impress

The idea is that a man who is being
carried away by the current of a river can
never be able to cross it by forcing his way
against the current; while he who moves
along the current by catching hold of a barge
or a log of wood or by floating on the
surface of water through the art of swimming
reaches the bank and even goes beyond it.
Similarly, the man who having been caught
in the stream of PrakГҰti struggles against it,
i.e., wilfully neglects his duties, cannot go
beyond Nature, but on the other hand gets
involved in it more and more; while he who
betaking himself to God or to Karmayoga
(the path of Action) or rising above PrakГҰti
according to the process of JВӨГҘnayoga,
continues to perform duties according to his
nature, is freed from the bondage of Karma
and transcends PrakГҰti, i.e., realizes God.
KuntвҲӮ, ArjunaГӯs mother, was a very brave
lady; while sending her message through вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a Himself, she urged the PГҘЖ’Е’avas to take

KuntвҲӮГӯs son, He seeks to impress on him that
being the son of a brave mother and a heroic
soul himself, he would not be able to keep aloof
from war.
Arjuna was a KВ¶atriya, fighting was his
natural duty; hence it was not sinful for him
to participate in war. Under such circumstances,
it was in no way desirable for him to seek to
avoid it. That he was nevertheless unwilling
to perform his natural duty in the shape of
fighting in a righteous cause showed nothing
but want of thought on his part; otherwise
there was no sufficient ground for his shirking
the duty. This is what the Lord seeks to
convey by the words Г«Kartum na icchasi yat
mohГҘtГӯ.
Again, fighting was a natural duty with
Arjuna; hence he was bound by it, i.e.,

it even against his will and impelled by his
nature he would have to do it. Therefore, if
he did it as the Lord bade him do, i.e., according
In the foregoing verses man has been declared as dependent on his nature in the matter
of performing actions. Here one may ask: PrakГҰti or nature is unconscious, how can it bring
anyone under its sway? The Lord, therefore, says:

=== PAGE 768 ===

* Chapter XVIII *
767
he dependГіon his own nature or on PrakГҰti
or God? For, at one place man has been
declared free as having the right to act (II.47),
and at another place as subject to his individual
nature (III.33) and at a third place as dependent
on God (X.8). In this very chapter he has
been declared as dependent on or subject to
his own peculiar nature in verses 59 and 60
and in the present verse as dependent on
God. This point evidently needs elucidation.
In this connection it may be submitted
that man is neither free to act nor to abstain
from action. That is why it has been said that
no living being can remain inactive even for
a moment (III.5). Even the statement that
man has the right to act is intended not to
declare him free but only to proclaim him as
dependent; for he has thereby been pronounced
as incapable of giving up action. Now as
regards the agency on which he depends, our
submission is that it is just the same to call
him as dependent on PrakГҰti, on his own
nature or on God. For, as a matter of fact,
PrakГҰti and nature are synonymous terms;
and as for God, He urges the different JвҲӮvas
or embodied souls to action through His
illusive power according to their individual
nature in quite an unconcerned way, i.e.,
remaining altogether unaffected or unattached.
Therefore, to call a man dependent on God is
just the same as to declare him subject to his
own nature. In the alternative case it is God
who is the lord and director of PrakГҰti, so that
even to call a man as subject to his own
Though the man who is seated in a
vehicle moves not himself, the motion of the
vehicle imparts locomotion to him; even so
though the Self is immovableГіis really
speaking wholly beyond all activity,Гіyet,
being identified with a body due to ignorance
that has existed from time without beginning,
the activity of the body is attributed to the
Soul. This is what the Lord means by
figuratively speaking of the body as a vehicle.
The driver who propels a vehicle remains
in the vehicle himself; even so God dwells in
the heart of all creatures and it is while seated
in their heart that He causes them to revolve
according to their Karma. Hence no decree
of God is liable to the least error; for all-
powerful, all-pervading and omniscient as
He is, He is fully aware of all the doings of
those creatures. This is what is meant by the
first half of this verse.
In order to reward or punish the various
JвҲӮvas or embodied souls according to the
doings of their previous lives, God causes
them to be born in different species. He
unites them with or disjoins them from various
objects, activities and living beings and urges
them to newer forms of activity in accordance
with their nature. This is how God causes
living beings to revolve through His MГҘyГҘ.
This raises the question of freedom of
will. Is man free to act according to his will
or does he depend for his actions on any

Here it may be asked: If man is absolutely
dependent, what is the way of redeeming
him and what need is there for scriptures
determining what a man should do and what
he should not. Our reply to this question is
that the вҖәГҘstras are not intended to divert a
man from his natural duties or to urge him
to acts which are antagonistic to his nature.
Their intention is to deter him from wrongful
acts which he is liable to commit under the
impulse of likes and dislikes while discharging
In the preceding verse it has been proved that man is not free to abandon his duties,
he is driven to his natural duties under the prompting of his own nature; for Almighty God,
who remains seated in the heart of all as their inner controller, causes all living beings to
revolve according to their own nature and it is not possible for man to resist His prompting.
The question, therefore, arises: If such is the case, what should a man do in order to get
rid of the bondage of Karma and attain supreme peace? Thereupon the Lord prescribes the
duty for Arjuna in the following verse:
Гғв—ҠпЈҝп¬Ӯ вүҘГҠГ”вҒ„UГЎГҠВў ВӘГ«ВҝU вҖҡп¬ӮВёГ·ГҠп¬ӮпЈҝЕё Г·ГҠвҒ„UГғвҖ“

The personal pronoun Г«TamГӯ refers to
the same almighty, all-supporting and all-
pervading God, who is the director and inner
controller of all and has been spoken of in
the preceding verse as seated in the heart of
all living beings. And he who, having offered
his mind, intellect, senses, and vital airs, as

Him, throws his entire responsibility on Him,
is said to have taken refuge in him with all
his being.
That is to say, having devoutly ascertained
the virtues, glory, truth and essential character
of God, such a man looks upon Him alone as
his supreme goal and safest retreat, chief
support and total wealth; and recognizing
Him as his lord, protector, supporter, director
and greatest wellwisher, he depends on Him
in everyway and is rid of all fear. Regarding
everything as belonging to God and feeling
His presence everywhere, he gives up the
feeling of mineness and the sense of doership,
as well as attachment and desire, in respect
of all actions and serves God, seated in the
heart of all living beings, according to His
behests through the performance of his duties.

or

The partcle Г«ItiГӯ in this verse marks the

The word Г«JВӨГҘnamГӯ covers the entire

revealed before the unqualified.
The personal pronoun Г«MayГҘГӯ has been
used by the Lord to show that none else
could expound the truth of His divine virtues,
glory and essential character as He Himself
would do; hence the wisdom taught by Him
above was exceedingly valuable. Even so

qualified to receive it. And the passive participle
Г«Г…khyГҘtamГӯ indicates that whatever the Lord
had got to say on the subject had already
been told; and that he had nothing more to
say.

All that the Lord has told Arjuna so far

In the preceding verse the Lord directed

Knowing the Lord as all-powerful, all-
supporting, all-wise, all-pervading, the ruler
and inner controller of all, and a veritable
ocean of surpassing beauty, love, divinity
and other virtues, he who steadily fixes his

A devotee of the Lord recognizes Him
as his only supporter, master, guardian, supreme
goal and chief support, and places himself
wholly under the LordГӯs control. He does not
reserve the least freedom to himself and entirely
depends on the Lord. He remains ever
contented with every dispensation of the Lord,
obeys Him under all circumstances, and loves
Him with an undivided heart and with utmost
reverence. This is what the Lord expects
Arjuna to do when He asks Arjuna to be
devoted to Him.
A votary of the Lord offers worship to
His image with reverence, devotion and love,
by means of leaves and flowers as mentioned
in verse 26 of Chapter IX. He invokes the
LordГӯs presence with his heart and worships
the Lord mentally. He revers the LordГӯs
utterances, the scene of His sports, and His
images in everyway; and realizing the LordГӯs
presence everywhere or treating all living beings

Arjuna was a beloved devotee and friend
of the Lord. Therefore, out of love and
kindly feelings towards him, the Lord was
anxious that Arjuna, and through him all
qualified souls, should repose unwavering
faith in the Lord. It is with this idea that He
uses the words Г¬Te satyam pratijГҘne (I truly

who pursues the course of discipline suggested
above surely realizes Him, a man should
strive his level best to attain the qualifications
mentioned in the verse.
The words Г¬Me priyaВЁ asiГ® are intended

in the words Г¬Te satyam pratijГҘneГ®. What He
means to say is that it was His love for Arjuna
that compelled Him to give the above assurance
in order to strengthen his faith; otherwise there
was no need for Him to do so.*

The personal pronoun Г«MГҘmГӯ in this

ideal of virtue. And He is the same who
manifested Himself in the form of a man-lion
in the Satyayuga and rescued PrahlГҘda.

Our object here is to show how great was the love the Lord bore towards Arjuna. This itself will
indicate how dearly he was loved by Arjuna.
BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a was a close companion of Arjuna and often remained by his side even during
his excursions into the forest and his sports in water, at the royal court as well as during sacrificial
performances etc. They were on such intimate terms with each other that even in their domestic life one
came across scenes of free interchange of pure and unadulterated love between them. Returning from the
camp of the PГҘЖ’Е’avas, SaВӨjaya, King DhГҰtarГҘВ¶ВўraГӯs minister, gave the following account to his master:Гі
Г¬I have seen unique love between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna. In order to have a talk with them I called

seat, clad in rich constumes and adorned with costly jewels. In the lap of Arjuna rested the feet of вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a,
while ArjunaГӯs own feet rested in the lap of DraupadвҲӮ (the common consort of the PГҘЖ’Е’avas) and SatyabhГҘmГҘ

beckoned to me take my seat on it. I touched it with reverence and squatted on the floor.Г®

KarЖ’a was in possession of an infallible weapon (a kind of lancet) which he had received from, Indra,

chapter. Laying down all these duties in God
in the manner suggested in the commentary
on the verses mentioned above is what is meant
by the verb Г«ParityajyaГӯ. For, while discussing

even for anything which is more valuable than the kingdom of the three worlds. Today I cannot contain
Myself for joy to see Arjuna reborn as it were.Г®

There are many more episodes illustrative of the ideal love between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna. For them

It was due to this exceptional love of Arjuna that the Lord had to unfold to him the most hidden secret

greatest Vedantists and exponents of the Vedas. After his ascent to heaven the pious king YudhiВ¶Вўhira, who

by taking refuge in the Lord. The idea has

The word Г«PГҘpaГӯ forming part of the
compound word Г«SarvapГҘpebhyaВЁГӯ in this
verse stands for the bondage of action in the
shape of fruits of good and evil deeds, under
which this JвҲӮva or embodied soul has been
revolving from birth to birth through different
species of life; and the absolution from sins
referred to here consists in freeing one from
the above bondage. Therefore, the idea
underlying the freedom from sins promised
by the Lord is the same as has been conveyed
in verse 31 of Chapter III by the words
Г«KarmabhiВЁ mucyanteГӯ by the words
Г«MГҰtyusaвүҲsГҘrasГҘgarГҘt samuddhartГҘ bhavГҘmi,
in verse 7 of Chapter XII, and by the words
Г«MatprasГҘdГҘt sarvadurgГҘЖ’вҲӮ tariВ¶yasiГӯ in verse

The Lord winds up the teaching of the
GвҲӮtГҘ by comforting Arjuna in the words Г¬MГҘ
вҖәucaВЁГ® (worry not). Concluding with these
words the teaching which He commenced in
verse 11 of Chapter II with the words
Г«AвҖ№ocyГҘnГӯ (not worth grieving for), the Lord
exhorts Arjuna, who had already betaken
himself to Him, to surrender himself completely
and thereby be rid of all worries, and having
shed all grief and fear, abandon himself to
His care for ever. This total absence of sorrow
and realization of God form the principal
theme of the GвҲӮtГҘ.
And having carefully listened to the GвҲӮtГҘ,
Arjuna himself not only expresses his
willingness to do His bidding in verse 73 of
the present chapter, but actually participated
in the MahГҘbhГҘrata war and thus discharged
his 
sacred 
obligation. 
Г«SarvadharmГҘn
parityajyaГӯ should, therefore, be taken to mean
laying down all oneГӯs duties in the Lord, and
not actually giving them up. And the man who
has laid down his duties in the Lord, regards
everything as belonging to Him; and
completely renouncing the feeling of Г«IГӯ and
Г«mineГӯ, as well as attachment and desire in
respect of his mind, senses and body and with
regard to all actions performed by them as well
as to their fruit, continues to perform such duties
only for His sake and according to His behests
and prompting like a mere tool in His hands.
Having resigned all his duties to God in
the above manner, the devotee who has solely
taken refuge in the Lord regards Him as his
supreme goal, safest retreat, chief support,
dearest object of love, greatest wellwisher, most
intimate friend and nearest relative, and looks
upon Him as his supporter, lord and guardian.
He constantly thinks of Him at all times with
supreme reverence and exclusive love even
while sitting or standing, awake or asleep,
eating and drinking, walking and moving from
one place to another and carrying out His
behests in every way. He ever remains
contented with His dispensation and like the
celebrated devotee, PrahlГҘda, entirely depends
on Him and Him alone. All this is covered
Having thus concluded the teaching of the GвҲӮtГҘ, the Lord now seeks to glorify the teaching
and the study etc. of this gospel and with this end in view He first points out the marks of him
who is unqualified for the same and forbids Arjuna to impart the teaching to such a man.

This secret gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ should never be imparted to a man who lacks in

=== PAGE 777 ===

776
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
austerity, nor to him who is wanting in devotion, nor even to him who is not willing
to hear; and in no case to him who finds fault with Me.
 (67)
who regards himself as everything. The most
esoteric gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ should not be
delivered to such a man either; for being
incapable of grasping its secret he would be
unable to assimilate it.
Even if a man practises austerity in the
shape of performing his sacred duties, but
having no reverence and love for the teaching
of the GвҲӮtГҘ does not care to lend his ear to it,
this most esoteric gospel should not be delivered
to him. For a man of this type would get
disgusted with it, and would not be able to
appreciate it. Thereby he would only belittle
the teaching as well as the Lord.
In no case should this teaching be related
to a man who cavils at the Lord,Гіwho has
assumed a form with attributes for redeeming
the world,Гіwho paints His virtues as a vice
and vilifies Him. For, being jealous of the
LordГӯs virtues, glory and divinity, he would
treat the Lord with even greater contempt
and thereby aggravate his sin.
He who is free from all the four
disqualifications mentioned in this verse is
unquestionably fully qualified to receive this
gospel. Next to him, he who lacks penance
in the form of devotion to his duty, but is free
from the other three disqualifications is also
eligible for it. And he too who is neither
given to austerity nor fully devoted to the
Lord, but who is willing to hear the GвҲӮtГҘ, is
qualified to a certain extent. He, however,
who looks on the Lord with a carping eye or
vilifies Him is absolutely unqualified.
The demonstrative pronoun Г«IdamГӯ in this
verse covers the entire range of the LordГӯs
teachings imparted to Arjuna from verse 11 of
Chapter II to the preceding verse with a view
to expounding the truth of His own virtues,
glory, mystery and essential character. In order
to determine the eligibility for receiving this
gospel the Lord forbids Arjuna to repeat it to
those who labour under the four disqualifications
mentioned in this verse. Out of the four types
of unqualified persons referred to above the
Lord mentions first of all him who lacks austerity.
By shutting out such a man from the portals of
the GвҲӮtГҘ, the Lord seeks to impress upon Arjuna
that the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ is an extremely
profound secret, that Arjuna was His most
loving devotee and endowed with divine
virtues, hence He had confided it to him in his
own interest, recognizing him as qualified to
receive it. Therefore He warns Arjuna not to
repeat the GвҲӮtГҘ, replete as it is with an exposition
of His virtues, glory and reality, to a man who
is not given to austerity in the form of discharging
his own sacred obligations, who having
abandoned his duty has given himself over to
sinful ways out of greed for worldly pleasures
due to attachment for sensuous enjoyments.
For such a man would be incapable of
assimilating this teaching and would thereby
bring dishonour to the same as well as to the
Lord Himself.
The compound word Г«AbhaktГҘyaГӯ stands
for the unbeliever who has no faith in God,
much less love or reverence for Him, and
Having thus pointed out the marks of him who is unqualified to receive the gospel of
the GвҲӮtГҘ, the Lord now tells Arjuna in the following two verses the reward and value of
propagating it among His devotees.

devotees; вҖўГҒГ·Е“ГҠSГҝГҒГғ will preach; Г·ГҒQВ§в—ҠЛңГ” love; в—ҠГҒГҝ to Me; В¬вҒ„UГҠв—ҠЛңГ” supreme; вҲ‘ГҺВ§Г п¬ӮГҠ having offered;

He who, offering the highest love to Me, preaches the most profound gospel of the
GвҲӮtГҘ among My devotees, shall come to Me alone; there is no doubt about it.
(68)
reverence for the Lord Himself or His
utterances, a devotee of God is overwhelmed
with love by the thought of His name, virtues,
sports, glory and essential character and
preaches the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ among His
devotees in a disinterested spirit for the sake
of His pleasure alone. That is to say, he gives
them lessons on the original text of the GвҲӮtГҘ,
explains the meaning of its verses, helps them
recite the text with correct intonation, brings
out and elucidates the ideas contained in it,
resolves the doubts of his audience and
inculcates the truth of the GвҲӮtГҘ upon their mind
and awakens in them a strong impulse to
translate into practice the teaching of the GвҲӮtГҘ.
All this is covered by preaching the gospel of
the  GвҲӮtГҘ among His devotees with supreme
devotion to Him.
A devotee of God, who propagates the
ideas of the Lord among those who are
qualified to receive them, in a disinterested
spirit and with the sole object of attaining
devotion to Him, doubtless reaches Him.
That is to say, the above is an unfailing
means to His realization; hence those qualified
devotees who seek to attain Him must
undertake the mission of preaching and
popularizing the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ.
The demonstrative adjective Г«ImamГӯ in
this verse refers to the entire gospel of the
GвҲӮtГҘ. The use of the adjectives Г«ParamamГӯ
and Г«GuhyamГӯ with reference to it is intended
to show that this teaching, inasmuch as it
secures freedom from the bondage of worldly
existence and leads one to realize God Himself,
is superb and esoteric.
Endowed with faith in God, His devotees
recognize Him as responsible for the  creation,
maintenance and destruction of the whole
universe, all-powerful and the universal Lord,
and love Him accordingly. They are eager to
hear of His virtues, glory, pastimes and reality,
and are delighted to hear the same. Such
devotees are referred to here by the term
Г«MadbhakteВ¶uГӯ. The use of this compound word
is intended to determine the eligibility for
receiving the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ. The idea is
that the devotee of God is automatically freed
from all the four disqualifications referred to
in the preceding verse. Therefore, a devotee
of the Lord is the only fit recipient of this gospel
and every person, to whatever grade in society
and to whatever caste he may belong, can be
a devotee of God (IX.32). Hence the caste,
creed and nationality etc., of a man are no
bar to his eligibility. Possessed of extreme

Among men there is none who does Me a more loving service than he; nor shall
anyone be dearer to Me in the entire world than he.                                (69)
The personal pronoun Г«TasmГҘtГӯ in this

and preaches its gospel among His devotees
as mentioned in the foregoing verses.
Dissemination of His ideas among His
devotees is dearer to the Lord than all other
duties that endear one to Him, such as the

* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
mentioned above, does not refer to the present
alone, it is equally true of the future as well.
That is to say, it is hardly possible that
anyone would be dearer to Him even at a
future date. For when no other service is
dearer to the Lord than the one in which he
is engaged, how could anyone be more
beloved of Him through any other practice ?
Therefore, of all the practices helpful to His
realization, the mission of devoutly propagating
His ideas among His devotees is the best. It
is with this belief that the Lord expects His
devotees to proceed with this noble work.
This is what He seeks to convey in the latter
half of the verse.
performance of sacrifices and penance and
practice of charity, service, worship, Japa
(the repetition of sacred formulas) and

dear to the Lord as the mission referred to

ideas among His devotees with reverence
and devotion is dearer to Him than anyone
else; none is dearer than he. Completely
neglecting his own interests, he does only
that which is dear to the Lord, hence he is
exceptionally dear to Him. This is what is
meant by the first half of this verse.
The proclamation of the Lord that no
one is dearer to Him than the devotee
In this way in the preceding two verses the Lord pointed out the reward and value  of
propagating among His devotees the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ in a reverent and devout spirit.
Everyone, however, is not equal to the task; scarce anyone is qualified for it. Hence He now
proceeds to glorify the study of the GвҲӮtГҘ.
вҖўГӨГҝпЈҝcГҝГғпЈҝ Гё Гҝ Гҹв—ҠВў Е“ГӘГҝZ вҖҡВўп¬ӮГҠЕ’в—ҠГҠп¬ӮГҝГҠпЈҝвҖ”вҖ“

The demonstrative adjective Г«ImamГӯ along
with the words Г«Г…vayoВЁ saвүҲvГҘdamГӯ here
refers to the text of the GвҲӮtГҘ in the form of a
dialogue between Arjuna and BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a, which has been characterized in verse
68 above as Г«Paramam guhyamГӯ (the most
esoteric gospel). The use of the adjective
Г«DharmyamГӯ with reference to it is intended
to convey that the gospel in question has
been delivered by the divine Lord Himself;
hence whatever has been taught therein is
replete with virtue from beginning to end. It
contains nothing which offends against
righteousness or which is superfluous.
Therefore, it is the paramount duty of man to
follow the teachings embodied in it.
Receiving lessons on the holy GвҲӮtГҘ from
the devotees of God who are conversant
with its inner meaning, reciting it daily, going
through its translation in oneГӯs own language,
pondering over its meaning and striving to
grasp the same with the help of those who
are in the know of it,Гіpractice of all these is
included in the study of this scripture.
Going through the translation of the GвҲӮtГҘ
side by side with reciting the text, and daily
recitation of the GвҲӮtГҘ with an eye to its meaning
is much better than going through the text or
daily reciting it without following its meaning;
and it is still better to be overwhelmed with
love and get imbued with its spirit while going
through or reciting the text in an intelligent way.

wisdom-sacrifice. The practice in the form of
this wisdom-sacrifice has been recognized as

The latter half of this verse is intended

aforesaid manner. The idea is that a study of
this scripture enables one to acquaint oneself
correctly and fully with the truth about the
absolute and relative, as well as the manifest
and unmanifest, aspects of the Divinity.
Therefore, whoever studies the GвҲӮtГҘ with the

Having thus pointed out the value of a study of the holy GвҲӮtГҘ, the Lord now declares
the reward of hearing it in the case of those who are unable to study it in the above manner.

To say nothing of him who preaches
the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ among others as
mentioned in verse 68, or of him who studies
it himself as referred to in verse 70 above,
even he who is merely able to hear it with
faith is absolved of sin. Therefore, he who is
unable to preach or even study it himself
should make it a point at least to hear it.
The man who is thus given to hearing the
GвҲӮtГҘ is rid of all sins committed in successive
previous lives,Гіwhich bring about oneГӯs birth
among low-born creatures such as beasts and
birds etc., and throw one into the infernal
regions,Гіand obtains a residence in the higher
worlds from IndraГӯs heaven onward to the
supreme abode of God according to the degree
of his faith and devotion. This is what is meant
by the latter half of this verse.
The use of the word Г«NaraВЁГӯ is intended
to show that he who lacks the inclination
even to hear with reverence the holy GвҲӮtГҘ
hardly deserves to be called a human being;
for his human birth is proving fruitless.
Therefore, he is only a beast in human form.
Believing in the existence of God as
well as in His virtues and glory, and with a
firm conviction that the holy GвҲӮtГҘ is the word
of God Himself, and that whatever has been
said therein is absolute truth, and reposing
faith in the exponent of the GвҲӮtГҘ, the man
who hears the original text or its exposition
with love and eagerness is referred to here by
the word Г«вҖәraddhГҘvГҘnГӯ. And Г«AnasВөuyaВЁГӯ is
he who does not find fault with the Lord or
His utterance nor shows disrespect in any
form to the holy GвҲӮtГҘ while hearing it.
Having thus glorified the preaching, recitation and hearing of the holy GвҲӮtГҘ, the Lord
now enquires of Arjuna his reactions to the above, just in order to awaken him to his real
situation, although He knew everything Himself.

he had preferred to live on alms abandoning

The above two questions of the Lord

should devote himself to the study and hearing

teaching of the Lord, so that it is essential to

The demonstrative pronoun Г«EtatГӯ in this
verse covers the entire range of the most
esoteric divine teaching imparted by the Lord
from verse 11 of Chapter II to verse 66 of the
present chapter. It is in order to impress upon
Arjuna the importance of this teaching that
the Lord asks him the question embodied in
the first half of this verse. The idea is that the
above teaching of the Lord is something very
rare; He could not declare His divinity to
everybody or ask anyone and everyone to
seek refuge in Him. He, therefore, was naturally
anxious to know whether Arjuna had carefully
and attentively heard His teachings; for should
he have missed it, he would have certainly
committed an egregious blunder.
Now, if Arjuna had carefully heard the
teaching, it must have borne its fruit. He,
therefore, reminded Arjuna how he had
complained of his being puzzled about his
duty (II.7), how he had reckoned it a sin to
discharge his sacred obligation (I.36), how
Thus enquired by the Lord, Arjuna conveys his reactions to Him, expressing his gratitude.

This is what he seeks to convey by addressing

about the virtues, glory and divinity of the

virtues, glory, divinity and essential character
of the Lord had been fully awakened, and
His integral being had been revealed to him,
leaving nothing unknown to him. This is
what Arjuna seeks to impress on the Lord by
the words Г«SmГҰtiВЁ LabdhГҘГӯ.
Arjuna had now no doubt left in his mind
Having thus repeated the holy GвҲӮtГҘ in the form of a dialogue between BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna in response to the enquiry from DhГҰtarГҘВ¶Вўra, SaВӨjaya now reveals to his
master the glory of the GвҲӮtГҘ in the following two verses while concluding his message.
вҖҡTГҝ В©Uп¬ӮГҠГё

Гё and; в—ҠвҖһUГҠГ в—ҠЕёвҖ” of the high-souled; вҖҡВўп¬ӮГҠЕ’в—ҠЛңГ” conversation; Гҹв—Ҡв—ҠЛңГ” this; вҖўГјГҠГҠГ’В·в—ҠЛңГ” heard; вҖўЕ’ЛңГ”Г·ГҲГғв—ҠЛңГ”
mysterious; вҒ„UГҠпЈҝв—ҠвҖһUВ·ВёГЎГҠв—ҠЛңГ” (and) thrilling (lit., making the hair stand on their ends).
SaВӨjaya said: Thus I heard the mysterious and thrilling conversation between вҖәrвҲӮ
KГҰВ¶Ж’a and the high-souled Arjuna, son of KuntвҲӮ.                                     (74)
The particle Г«ItiГӯ marks the conclusion
of the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ.
The use of the epithet Г«VГҘsudevaГӯ (lit.,
the all-pervading Spirit) for вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and of
the qualification Г«MahГҘtmГҘГӯ (high-souled or
noble-minded) with reference to Arjuna is
intended to glorify the GвҲӮtГҘ. The idea is that
this gospel has been preached by вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a,
who is no other than the all-pervading Divinity,
the indweller of all hearts, in response to the
enquiry from Arjuna, who is an incarnation
of the divine sage Nara; hence it is highly
valuable. No other sacred teaching can bear
comparison with it; for it is the quintessence
of all scriptures or sacred books.*
The dialogue between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and
* ВӘГӢГғГҠ вҖҡГҲВӘГӢГғГҠ вҲ‘В§ГғВёвҲҡГҝГҠ ГҒвҲ‘В§в—ҠГЈГҝГ’вҖ” вүҘГҠГҠSГІГҠГ”ГҒп¬ӮSГғвҒ„Г’вҖ”вҖ“ ГҝГҠ Sп¬ӮГҝВў В¬kЕёГҠГ·SГҝ в—ҠГҲПҖВ¬kГҠГҒmГҒЕёвҖ”вҖҡГҺГғГҠH (MahГҘ., BhвҲӮВ¶ma, 43.1)
Г¬The GвҲӮtГҘ alone should be heard and chanted, studied and taught, as well as pondered and assimilated;
collections of other scriptures are of no avail. For the GвҲӮtГҘ has flowed from the lotus-like lips of no other
than BhagavГҘn ViВ¶Ж’u, who bears a lotus on His navel.Г®

=== PAGE 783 ===

782
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
Arjuna has been spoken of as mysterious
because it enables one to realize the integral
Divinity, possessed of wonderful and
transcendent virtues, glory and supernatural
powers. And even as a man hears and
understands it, he is filled with delight and
wonder, which make his hair stand on end.
Hence it has been called Г«RomaharВ¶aЖ’amГӯ.
SaВӨjaya feels proud that he has been able to
hear with his own ears such a wonderful dialogue.
вҲҡГҝГҠвҖҡВ¬ЛқвҖҡГҠЕ’ГҠГ«ВҝЛӣвҖ°UГғп¬ӮГҠЕёпЈҝГғeГҲsГ”в—ҠвҖһВўU 
В¬вҒ„Uв—ҠЛңГ”вҖ“
ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘВў ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝEвҒ„UГҠГ вҲ‘ГҺВ§cГЎГҠГҠГ вҖҡГҠЛҮГҠГҠГ вҲ‘В§Г•ГҝГғвҖ” Sп¬ӮГҝв—ҠЛңГ”H 75H
вҲҡГҝГҠвҖҡВ¬ЛқвҖҡГҠЕ’ГҠГғЛңГ” (having been blessed with the divine vision) by the grace of вҖәrвҲӮ VyГҘsa;
ГјГҠГҲГғп¬ӮГҠЕёЛңГ” have heard; вҲһГғГғЛңГ” this; ВӘГҲsГ”в—ҠЛңГ” esoteric; вҖўвҖһUв—ҠЛңГ” I; В¬вҒ„Uв—ҠЛңГ” supremely; ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘв—ҠЛңГ” Yoga or wisdom;
ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝEвҒ„UГҠГғЛңГ” from BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a; вҲ‘ГҺВ§cГЎГҠГҠГғЛңГ” the Lord of Yoga; вҖҡГҠЛҮГҠГҠГғЛңГ” in person; вҲ‘В§Г•ГҝГғвҖ”
imparting (it to Arjuna); Sп¬ӮГҝв—ҠЛңГ” Himself.
Having been blessed with the divine vision by the grace of вҖәrвҲӮ VyГҘsa, I heard in
person this supremely esoteric gospel from the Lord of Yoga, вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a Himself,
imparting it to Arjuna.
 (75)
The use of the compound word
Г«VyГҘsaprasГҘdГҘtГӯ is intended to express
SaВӨjayaГӯs gratitude towards MaharВ¶i VyГҘsa,
who had so kindly gifted him with the divine
vision, i.e., with the supernatural faculties of
seeing what is out of sight, hearing what is
out of hearing and grasping what is out of
oneГӯs mental grasp. It was due to that
supernatural gift that SaВӨjaya was enabled to
hear the divine gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ, which he
was otherwise incapable of hearing.
The demonstrative pronoun Г«EtatГӯ stands
here for the holy GвҲӮtГҘ in the form of the
above dialogue between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna.
The use of the adjective Г«ParamamГӯ with
reference to it is intended to bring out its
superb character; and Г«GuhyamГӯ points to its
esoteric nature, i.e., bangs its doors against
the unqualified. The third adjective Г«YogamГӯ
indicates that the gospel contains a detailed
exposition 
of 
Karmayoga, 
JВӨГҘnayoga,
DhyГҘnayoga, Bhaktiyoga and other practices
leading to God-realization; and the gospel
itself, i.e., even its devout recitation is an
independent means of realizing God and hence
deserves the title of Г«YogaГӯ.
Lastly, SaВӨjaya tells DhГҰtarГҘВ¶Вўra that the
GвҲӮtГҘ, which he had just repeated to the King,
had not been received by him through a third
agency but directly from the lotus-like lips of
the almighty Lord вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, the controller of
all Yogic powers.
Having thus revealed the value of hearing the most rare gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ, SaВӨjaya,
while expressing his own reactions, glorifies the recollection of that gospel.
вҒ„UГҠВЎГЈвҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ вҖҡВўп¬ӮГҠЕ’ГҒв—Ҡв—Ҡв—ҠjГҲГғв—ҠЛңГ”вҖ“
вҲ‘пЈҝВ§вүҘГҠп¬ӮГҠВЎВёГҲЕёГҝГҠпЈҝвҖ” В¬ГҲГЎГҝВў NUcГҝГҠГҒв—Ҡ Гё в—ҠГҲвҖһГҲUв—ҠГҲВёвҖһГҲUвҖ”H 76H
вҒ„UГҠВЎЕёЛңГ” O king; вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ recollecting again and again; вҖҡВўп¬ӮГҠЕ’в—ҠЛңГ” conversation; Гҹв—Ҡв—ҠЛңГ” this;
вҖўjГҲГғв—ҠЛңГ” marvellous; вҲ‘пЈҝВ§вүҘГҠп¬ӮГҠВЎВёГҲЕёГҝГҠпЈҝвҖ” between BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna; В¬ГҲГЎГҝв—ҠЛңГ” sacred; NUcГҝГҠГҒв—Ҡ
I rejoice; Гё and; в—ҠГҲвҖһГҲUв—ҠГҲВёвҖһГҲUвҖ” again and yet again.
Remembering, over and over, that sacred and mystic conversation between
BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna, O King ! I rejoice again and yet again.        (76)

=== PAGE 784 ===

* Chapter XVIII *
783
By giving his own reactions in this
verse SaВӨjaya glorifies the recollection of
the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ. SaВӨjaya means to
say that the gospel preached by the Lord
had captivated his heart so much that he had
lost his relish for anything else. He was
reminded of that teaching again and again,
and merged in the thought of it. He was
being transported with joy and overwhelmed
with love.
The holy GвҲӮtГҘ in the form of this mystic
conversation between BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a
and Arjuna purifies him who studies, teaches,
hears, ponders and discourses on it, and
does good to his soul in every way. Hence
it has been characterized as Г«PuЖ’yamГӯ (sacred).
And it reveals the marvellous virtues, glory,
divinity, truth, mystery and essential character
of the Lord; hence it has been spoken of as
Г«AdbhutamГӯ.
Having thus glorified the recollection of the GвҲӮtГҘ, SaВӨjaya while giving his further
reactions extols the recollection of the Cosmic Body of the Lord.
ГғГ¬ГҠ вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ MВ§В¬в—ҠГ ГҝjГҲГғВў вҖһUвҒ„пЈҝUвҖ”вҖ“
ГҒп¬ӮSв—ҠГҝГҠпЈҝ в—ҠпЈҝ в—ҠвҖһUГҠГЈвҒ„UГҠВЎГЈNUcГҝГҠГҒв—Ҡ Гё В¬ГҲЕёвҖ” В¬ГҲЕёвҖ”H 77H
ГғГғЛңГ” that;Гё and;  вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ вҖҡВўSв—ҠГҺГ Гҝ remembering again and again; MВ§В¬в—ҠЛңГ” form; вҖўГҒГ ГҠ вҖўjГҲГғв—ҠЛңГ” most
wonderful; вҖһUвҒ„пЈҝUвҖ” of вҖәrвҲӮ Hari; ГҒп¬ӮSв—ҠГҝвҖ” в—ҠпЈҝ (is) my wonder; в—ҠвҖһUГҠЕёЛңГ” great; вҒ„UГҠВЎЕёЛңГ” O king; NUcГҝГҠГҒв—Ҡ I rejoice;
Гё also; В¬ГҲЕёвҖ” В¬ГҲЕёвҖ” again and yet again.
Remembering also, again and again, that most wonderful form of вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, great
is my wonder and I rejoice over and over again.
(77)
By hearing, pondering and singing the
virtues, glory, exploits, divinity, greatness
and the sacred names of BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a,
and by perceiving and touching His Form a
man is rid of all sins. Association of any kind
with Him frees one from all sins, ignorance
and sorrows and the Lord robs the devotee
of his heart; hence He is termed as Г«HariГӯ.
The word Г«RВөupamГӯ qualified by the
adjectives Г«TatГӯ and Г«AtyadbhutamГӯ refers to
the most wonderful and divine Cosmic Body
of the Lord, which He had revealed before
Arjuna and the glory of whose vision has
been declared by the Lord Himself in verses
47 and 48 of Chapter XI.
The memory of that Cosmic Body of
the Lord did not fade from the mind of
SaВӨjaya, he was reminded of it again and
again and wondered how he was vouchsafed
a vision of that most rare and divine Form of
the Lord. He had no merit to his credit,
which could earn him that unique privilege.
The motiveless mercy of the Lord alone had
entitled him to that privilege. A mental survey
of the wonderful phenomena and events
forming part of that vision filled him with
wonder and awe at the marvellous Yogic
power of the Lord.
Thus giving his own reactions and glorifying the gospel of the GвҲӮtГҘ and the recollection
of the marvellous Form of the Lord, SaВӨjaya now concludes this chapter indicating the
certain prospect of the PГҘЖ’davaГӯs victory.
ГҝГІГҠ ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝEвҒ„UвҖ” вҲ‘ГҺВ§cГЎГҠГҠпЈҝ ГҝГІГҠ В¬ГҠГ•ГҠпЈҝВё Е“ЕёГҲЕ“ВёвҒ„UвҖ”вҖ“
ГғГІГҠ ГјГҠГӢГҢп¬ӮВЎГҝГҠпЈҝ Г·ГҚГҒГғЕ“ЛқГҲВёп¬ӮГҠ ЕёГӢГҒГғв—ҠВёГҒГғв—ҠВёв—ҠH 78H
ГҝГІГҠ where; ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘпЈҝEвҒ„UвҖ” the Lord of Yoga; вҲ‘ГҺВ§cГЎГҠвҖ” BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a; ГҝГІГҠ (and) where;

=== PAGE 785 ===

784
* Bhagavad-GвҲӮtВөa *
В¬ГҠГ•ВёвҖ” the son of PГҰthГҘ (KuntвҲӮ), Arjuna; Е“ЕёГҲЕ“ВёвҒ„UвҖ” the wielder of the GГҘЖ’Е’вҲӮva bow; ГғГІГҠ there;
ГјГҠГӢвҖ” glory; ГҒп¬ӮВЎГҝвҖ” victory; Г·ГҚГҒГғвҖ” prosperity; Е“ЛқГҲп¬ӮГҠ ЕёГӢГҒГғвҖ” (and) unfailing righteousness; в—ҠГҒГғвҖ” в—Ҡв—Ҡ
(such is) my conviction.
Wherever there is BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, the Lord of Yoga, and wherever there is
Arjuna, the wielder of the GГҘЖ’Е’вҲӮva bow, glory, victory, prosperity and unfailing
righteousness will surely be there: such is my conviction.                                         (78)
By using the epithet Г«YogeвҖ№varaВЁГӯ for
вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Г«DhanurdharaВЁГӯ for Arjuna,
SaВӨjaya impresses upon King DhГҰtarГҘВ¶tra the
glory of BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna, and
indicates the sure prospect of the PГҘЖ’Е’avaГӯs
victory in order to awaken in the kingГӯs mind
an inclination to negotiate peace. In other words,
SaВӨjaya wanted to make it clear that BhagavГҘn
вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a was the Lord of all Yogic powers
and that He was capable of creating,
maintaining and destroying the whole universe
in a trice by dint of His Yogic power. There
could be no doubt about the victory of King
YudhiВ¶Вўhira, who had BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a,
the manifest Divinity, as his supporter.
Besides this, Arjuna too was an
incarnation of the divine sage Nara, a beloved
friend of the Lord, and a great hero, the
wielder of the famous GГҘЖ’Е’вҲӮva bow; he too
had girded his loins to see his brother victorious.
Hence there was none who could vie with
YudhiВ¶Вўhira at that moment. For where the
sun is, light must be there; even so, the very
presence of BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna
ensures all glory, victory and righteousness.
And laurels go to him who has virtue on his
side. Therefore, the victory of the PГҘЖ’Е’avas
was guaranteed. The welfare of DhГҰtarГҘВ¶Вўra
lay in bringing round his sons to seek peace
with the PГҘЖ’Е’avas.
Лҷ ГғГ вҖҡГҒЕ’ГҒГғ ГјГҠГӢв—ҠjВӘп¬ӮeГӢГғГҠвҖҡГҚВ¬ГҒЕёВ·Г вҖҡГҲ вҖҷЛқrГҠГ”ГҒп¬ӮlГҠГҝГҠВў ГҝГҠпЈҝВӘвүҘГҠГҠSГІГҠпЈҝ ГјГҠГӢвҲ‘ГҺВ§cГЎГҠГҠВЎГҲВёЕёвҖҡВўп¬ӮГҠЕ’пЈҝ
в—ҠГҠпЈҝЛҮГҠвҖҡГӣГҝГҠвҖҡГҝГҠпЈҝВӘГҠпЈҝ ЕёГҠв—ҠГҠCГ”UГҠЕ’вүҘГҠГҠпЈҝЛҳГӨГҝГҠГҝвҖ”H 18H
Thus, in the UpaniВ¶ad sung by the Lord, the science of Brahma, the scripture
of Yoga, the dialogue between вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a and Arjuna, ends the eighteenth
chapter entitled Г¬The Yoga of Liberation through the path of
Knowledge and Self-surrender.Г®
The BhagavadgвҲӮtГҘ is the divine word of BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a, the supreme PuruВ¶ottama
or God Himself, who is adored by the whole animate and inanimate kingdom, is full of the
six divine properties, and is Bliss and Consciousness solidified. The scripture is a mine of
endless mysteries. It is by the grace of the supremely compassionate BhagavГҘn вҖәrвҲӮ KГҰВ¶Ж’a that
its inner meaning can be partially understood. He alone, who ponders the GвҲӮtГҘ with a heart
brimming over with supreme reverence and pure loving devotion, directly perceives the
grace of the Lord and can obtain a glimpse of the real character of the GвҲӮtГҘ. Therefore,
seekers of blessedness would do well to place before them the ideal of Arjuna, the prince
among devotees, and cultivating his divine virtues should hear, study and ruminate over the
GвҲӮtГҘ with faith and devotion; and they should whole-heartedly devote themselves to spiritual
practice according to their individual capacity in pursuance of the LordГӯs behests. Those who
proceed on the above lines are inspired with ever new thoughts and feelings of a supremely
blissful, unique and transcendent character. Attaining absolute purity of mind and enjoying
the exceptional grace of the Lord, they speedily realize Him.
U
